verses.en: And He said: "Take, I pray, your son, your only one, whom you love — Yitschak — and go, yourself, to the land of Moriyyah, and bring him up there as an ascent-offering upon one of the mountains which I shall say to you."

verses.he: וַ/יַּשְׁכֵּ֨ם אַבְרָהָ֜ם בַּ/בֹּ֗קֶר וַֽ/יַּחֲבֹשׁ֙ אֶת חֲמֹר֔/וֹ וַ/יִּקַּ֞ח אֶת שְׁנֵ֤י נְעָרָי/ו֙ אִתּ֔/וֹ וְ/אֵ֖ת יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֑/וֹ וַ/יְבַקַּע֙ עֲצֵ֣י עֹלָ֔ה וַ/יָּ֣קָם וַ/יֵּ֔לֶךְ אֶל הַ/מָּק֖וֹם אֲשֶׁר אָֽמַר ל֥/וֹ הָ/אֱלֹהִֽים
verses.en: And Avraham rose early in the morning and saddled his donkey, and took his two young men with him and Yitschak his son; and he split wood for the ascent-offering, and rose and went unto the place of which God had spoken to him.

verses.he: בַּ/יּ֣וֹם הַ/שְּׁלִישִׁ֗י וַ/יִּשָּׂ֨א אַבְרָהָ֧ם אֶת עֵינָ֛י/ו וַ/יַּ֥רְא אֶת הַ/מָּק֖וֹם מֵ/רָחֹֽק
verses.en: On the third day Avraham lifted up his eyes and saw the place from afar.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֶל נְעָרָ֗י/ו שְׁבוּ לָ/כֶ֥ם פֹּה֙ עִֽם הַ/חֲמ֔וֹר וַ/אֲנִ֣י וְ/הַ/נַּ֔עַר נֵלְכָ֖ה עַד כֹּ֑ה וְ/נִֽשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֖ה וְ/נָשׁ֥וּבָה אֲלֵי/כֶֽם
verses.en: And Avraham said unto his young men: "Stay here with the donkey, and I and the youth shall go yonder, and we shall bow down, and we shall return to you."

verses.he: וַ/יִּקַּ֨ח אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֶת עֲצֵ֣י הָ/עֹלָ֗ה וַ/יָּ֨שֶׂם֙ עַל יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֔/וֹ וַ/יִּקַּ֣ח בְּ/יָד֔/וֹ אֶת הָ/אֵ֖שׁ וְ/אֶת הַֽ/מַּאֲכֶ֑לֶת וַ/יֵּלְכ֥וּ שְׁנֵי/הֶ֖ם יַחְדָּֽו
verses.en: And Avraham took the wood of the ascent-offering and placed it upon Yitschak his son; and he took in his hand the fire and the knife; and the two of them went together.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יִצְחָ֜ק אֶל אַבְרָהָ֤ם אָבִי/ו֙ וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אָבִ֔/י וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר הִנֶּ֣נִּֽ/י בְנִ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר הִנֵּ֤ה הָ/אֵשׁ֙ וְ/הָ֣/עֵצִ֔ים וְ/אַיֵּ֥ה הַ/שֶּׂ֖ה לְ/עֹלָֽה
verses.en: And Yitschak spoke unto Avraham his father and said: "My father!" And he said: "Here I am, my son." And he said: "Behold the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for the ascent-offering?"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ אַבְרָהָ֔ם אֱלֹהִ֞ים יִרְאֶה לּ֥/וֹ הַ/שֶּׂ֛ה לְ/עֹלָ֖ה בְּנִ֑/י וַ/יֵּלְכ֥וּ שְׁנֵי/הֶ֖ם יַחְדָּֽו
verses.en: And Avraham said: "God will see to Himself the lamb for the ascent-offering, my son." And the two of them went together.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֗אוּ אֶֽל הַ/מָּקוֹם֮ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָֽמַר ל֣/וֹ הָ/אֱלֹהִים֒ וַ/יִּ֨בֶן שָׁ֤ם אַבְרָהָם֙ אֶת הַ/מִּזְבֵּ֔חַ וַֽ/יַּעֲרֹ֖ךְ אֶת הָ/עֵצִ֑ים וַֽ/יַּעֲקֹד֙ אֶת יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֔/וֹ וַ/יָּ֤שֶׂם אֹת/וֹ֙ עַל הַ/מִּזְבֵּ֔חַ מִ/מַּ֖עַל לָ/עֵצִֽים
verses.en: And they came to the place of which God had spoken to him; and Avraham built there the altar and arranged the wood; and he bound Yitschak his son and placed him upon the altar, atop the wood.

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁלַ֤ח אַבְרָהָם֙ אֶת יָד֔/וֹ וַ/יִּקַּ֖ח אֶת הַֽ/מַּאֲכֶ֑לֶת לִ/שְׁחֹ֖ט אֶת בְּנֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Avraham stretched out his hand and took the knife to slaughter his son.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֨א אֵלָ֜י/ו מַלְאַ֤ךְ יְהוָה֙ מִן הַ/שָּׁמַ֔יִם וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אַבְרָהָ֣ם אַבְרָהָ֑ם וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר הִנֵּֽנִ/י
verses.en: And a messenger of the LORD called unto him from the heavens and said: "Avraham! Avraham!" And he said: "Here I am."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר אַל תִּשְׁלַ֤ח יָֽדְ/ךָ֙ אֶל הַ/נַּ֔עַר וְ/אַל תַּ֥עַשׂ ל֖/וֹ מְא֑וּמָּה כִּ֣י עַתָּ֣ה יָדַ֗עְתִּי כִּֽי יְרֵ֤א אֱלֹהִים֙ אַ֔תָּה וְ/לֹ֥א חָשַׂ֛כְתָּ אֶת בִּנְ/ךָ֥ אֶת יְחִידְ/ךָ֖ מִמֶּֽ/נִּי
verses.en: And he said: "Do not stretch out your hand unto the youth, and do nothing to him; for now I know that you are a fearer of God, and you have not withheld your son, your only one, from Me."

verses.he: וַ/יִּשָּׂ֨א אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֶת עֵינָ֗י/ו וַ/יַּרְא֙ וְ/הִנֵּה אַ֔יִל אַחַ֕ר נֶאֱחַ֥ז בַּ/סְּבַ֖ךְ בְּ/קַרְנָ֑י/ו וַ/יֵּ֤לֶךְ אַבְרָהָם֙ וַ/יִּקַּ֣ח אֶת הָ/אַ֔יִל וַ/יַּעֲלֵ֥/הוּ לְ/עֹלָ֖ה תַּ֥חַת בְּנֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Avraham lifted up his eyes and saw — and behold, a ram behind, caught in the thicket by its horns. And Avraham went and took the ram and brought it up as an ascent-offering in place of his son.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֧א אַבְרָהָ֛ם שֵֽׁם הַ/מָּק֥וֹם הַ/ה֖וּא יְהוָ֣ה יִרְאֶ֑ה אֲשֶׁר֙ יֵאָמֵ֣ר הַ/יּ֔וֹם בְּ/הַ֥ר יְהוָ֖ה יֵרָאֶֽה
verses.en: And Avraham called the name of that place "The LORD Sees"; as it is said to this day: "On the mountain of the LORD, it shall be seen."

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֛א מַלְאַ֥ךְ יְהוָ֖ה אֶל אַבְרָהָ֑ם שֵׁנִ֖ית מִן הַ/שָּׁמָֽיִם
verses.en: And a messenger of the LORD called unto Avraham a second time from the heavens.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר בִּ֥/י נִשְׁבַּ֖עְתִּי נְאֻם יְהוָ֑ה כִּ֗י יַ֚עַן אֲשֶׁ֤ר עָשִׂ֨יתָ֙ אֶת הַ/דָּבָ֣ר הַ/זֶּ֔ה וְ/לֹ֥א חָשַׂ֖כְתָּ אֶת בִּנְ/ךָ֥ אֶת יְחִידֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And he said: "By Myself I have sworn — declares the LORD — because you have done this thing and have not withheld your son, your only one:

verses.he: כִּֽי בָרֵ֣ךְ אֲבָרֶכְ/ךָ֗ וְ/הַרְבָּ֨ה אַרְבֶּ֤ה אֶֽת זַרְעֲ/ךָ֙ כְּ/כוֹכְבֵ֣י הַ/שָּׁמַ֔יִם וְ/כַ/ח֕וֹל אֲשֶׁ֖ר עַל שְׂפַ֣ת הַ/יָּ֑ם וְ/יִרַ֣שׁ זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ אֵ֖ת שַׁ֥עַר אֹיְבָֽי/ו
verses.en: that I shall bless, yes, bless you, and I shall multiply, yes, multiply your seed as the stars of the heavens and as the sand that is upon the lip of the sea; and your seed shall possess the gate of its enemies.

verses.he: וְ/הִתְבָּרֲכ֣וּ בְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ כֹּ֖ל גּוֹיֵ֣י הָ/אָ֑רֶץ עֵ֕קֶב אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁמַ֖עְתָּ בְּ/קֹלִֽ/י
verses.en: And all the nations of the earth shall be blessed through your seed, because you have heard My voice."

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֤שָׁב אַבְרָהָם֙ אֶל נְעָרָ֔י/ו וַ/יָּקֻ֛מוּ וַ/יֵּלְכ֥וּ יַחְדָּ֖ו אֶל בְּאֵ֣ר שָׁ֑בַע וַ/יֵּ֥שֶׁב אַבְרָהָ֖ם בִּ/בְאֵ֥ר שָֽׁבַע
verses.en: And Avraham returned unto his young men, and they rose and went together to Be'er-Sheva; and Avraham dwelt in Be'er-Sheva.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י אַחֲרֵי֙ הַ/דְּבָרִ֣ים הָ/אֵ֔לֶּה וַ/יֻּגַּ֥ד לְ/אַבְרָהָ֖ם לֵ/אמֹ֑ר הִ֠נֵּה יָלְדָ֨ה מִלְכָּ֥ה גַם הִ֛וא בָּנִ֖ים לְ/נָח֥וֹר אָחִֽי/ךָ
verses.en: And it was after these things that it was told to Avraham, saying: "Behold, Milkah has also borne sons to Nachor your brother:

verses.he: אֶת ע֥וּץ בְּכֹר֖/וֹ וְ/אֶת בּ֣וּז אָחִ֑י/ו וְ/אֶת קְמוּאֵ֖ל אֲבִ֥י אֲרָֽם
verses.en: Uts his firstborn, and Buz his brother, and Qemu'el the father of Aram,

verses.he: וְ/אֶת כֶּ֣שֶׂד וְ/אֶת חֲז֔וֹ וְ/אֶת פִּלְדָּ֖שׁ וְ/אֶת יִדְלָ֑ף וְ/אֵ֖ת בְּתוּאֵֽל
verses.en: and Kesed, and Chazo, and Pildash, and Yidlaph, and Betu'el."

verses.he: וּ/בְתוּאֵ֖ל יָלַ֣ד אֶת רִבְקָ֑ה שְׁמֹנָ֥ה אֵ֨לֶּה֙ יָלְדָ֣ה מִלְכָּ֔ה לְ/נָח֖וֹר אֲחִ֥י אַבְרָהָֽם
verses.en: And Betu'el begot Rivqah. These eight Milkah bore to Nachor, the brother of Avraham.

verses.he: וּ/פִֽילַגְשׁ֖/וֹ וּ/שְׁמָ֣/הּ רְאוּמָ֑ה וַ/תֵּ֤לֶד גַּם הִוא֙ אֶת טֶ֣בַח וְ/אֶת גַּ֔חַם וְ/אֶת תַּ֖חַשׁ וְ/אֶֽת מַעֲכָֽה
verses.en: And his concubine, whose name was Re'umah, she also bore: Tevach, and Gacham, and Tachash, and Ma'akhah.

verses.he: וַ/יִּהְיוּ֙ חַיֵּ֣י שָׂרָ֔ה מֵאָ֥ה שָׁנָ֛ה וְ/עֶשְׂרִ֥ים שָׁנָ֖ה וְ/שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֑ים שְׁנֵ֖י חַיֵּ֥י שָׂרָֽה
verses.en: And the life of Sarah was a hundred years and twenty years and seven years — the years of the life of Sarah.

verses.he: וַ/תָּ֣מָת שָׂרָ֗ה בְּ/קִרְיַ֥ת אַרְבַּ֛ע הִ֥וא חֶבְר֖וֹן בְּ/אֶ֣רֶץ כְּנָ֑עַן וַ/יָּבֹא֙ אַבְרָהָ֔ם לִ/סְפֹּ֥ד לְ/שָׂרָ֖ה וְ/לִ/בְכֹּתָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And Sarah died in Qiryat-Arba, which is Chevron, in the land of Kena'an; and Avraham came to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֨קָם֙ אַבְרָהָ֔ם מֵ/עַ֖ל פְּנֵ֣י מֵת֑/וֹ וַ/יְדַבֵּ֥ר אֶל בְּנֵי חֵ֖ת לֵ/אמֹֽר
verses.en: And Avraham rose from before the face of his dead and spoke unto the sons of Chet, saying:

verses.he: גֵּר וְ/תוֹשָׁ֥ב אָנֹכִ֖י עִמָּ/כֶ֑ם תְּנ֨וּ לִ֤/י אֲחֻזַּת קֶ֨בֶר֙ עִמָּ/כֶ֔ם וְ/אֶקְבְּרָ֥ה מֵתִ֖/י מִ/לְּ/פָנָֽ/י
verses.en: "A stranger and a sojourner am I with you. Give me a holding of burial with you, that I may bury my dead from before my face."

verses.he: וַ/יַּעֲנ֧וּ בְנֵי חֵ֛ת אֶת אַבְרָהָ֖ם לֵ/אמֹ֥ר לֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And the sons of Chet answered Avraham, saying to him:

verses.he: שְׁמָעֵ֣/נוּ אֲדֹנִ֗/י נְשִׂ֨יא אֱלֹהִ֤ים אַתָּה֙ בְּ/תוֹכֵ֔/נוּ בְּ/מִבְחַ֣ר קְבָרֵ֔י/נוּ קְבֹ֖ר אֶת מֵתֶ֑/ךָ אִ֣ישׁ מִמֶּ֔/נּוּ אֶת קִבְר֛/וֹ לֹֽא יִכְלֶ֥ה מִמְּ/ךָ֖ מִ/קְּבֹ֥ר מֵתֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: "Hear us, my lord: a prince of God are you in our midst. In the choicest of our burial places bury your dead; not a man among us shall withhold his burial place from you, from burying your dead."

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֧קָם אַבְרָהָ֛ם וַ/יִּשְׁתַּ֥חוּ לְ/עַם הָ/אָ֖רֶץ לִ/בְנֵי חֵֽת
verses.en: And Avraham rose and bowed to the people of the land, to the sons of Chet.

verses.he: וַ/יְדַבֵּ֥ר אִתָּ֖/ם לֵ/אמֹ֑ר אִם יֵ֣שׁ אֶֽת נַפְשְׁ/כֶ֗ם לִ/קְבֹּ֤ר אֶת מֵתִ/י֙ מִ/לְּ/פָנַ֔/י שְׁמָע֕וּ/נִי וּ/פִגְעוּ לִ֖/י בְּ/עֶפְר֥וֹן בֶּן צֹֽחַר
verses.en: And he spoke with them, saying: "If it is your desire to bury my dead from before my face, hear me and entreat for me Efron son of Tsochar,

verses.he: וְ/יִתֶּן לִ֗/י אֶת מְעָרַ֤ת הַ/מַּכְפֵּלָה֙ אֲשֶׁר ל֔/וֹ אֲשֶׁ֖ר בִּ/קְצֵ֣ה שָׂדֵ֑/הוּ בְּ/כֶ֨סֶף מָלֵ֜א יִתְּנֶ֥/נָּה לִ֛/י בְּ/תוֹכְ/כֶ֖ם לַ/אֲחֻזַּת קָֽבֶר
verses.en: that he may give me the cave of Makhpelah which is his, which is at the edge of his field; for its full price let him give it to me in your midst, as a holding of burial."

verses.he: וְ/עֶפְר֥וֹן יֹשֵׁ֖ב בְּ/ת֣וֹךְ בְּנֵי חֵ֑ת וַ/יַּעַן֩ עֶפְר֨וֹן הַ/חִתִּ֤י אֶת אַבְרָהָם֙ בְּ/אָזְנֵ֣י בְנֵי חֵ֔ת לְ/כֹ֛ל בָּאֵ֥י שַֽׁעַר עִיר֖/וֹ לֵ/אמֹֽר
verses.en: And Efron was sitting among the sons of Chet; and Efron the Chittite answered Avraham in the hearing of the sons of Chet, of all who came through the gate of his city, saying:

verses.he: לֹֽא אֲדֹנִ֣/י שְׁמָעֵ֔/נִי הַ/שָּׂדֶה֙ נָתַ֣תִּי לָ֔/ךְ וְ/הַ/מְּעָרָ֥ה אֲשֶׁר בּ֖/וֹ לְ/ךָ֣ נְתַתִּ֑י/הָ לְ/עֵינֵ֧י בְנֵי עַמִּ֛/י נְתַתִּ֥י/הָ לָּ֖/ךְ קְבֹ֥ר מֵתֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: "No, my lord, hear me: the field I give to you, and the cave that is in it — to you I give it; in the eyes of the sons of my people I give it to you. Bury your dead."

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁתַּ֨חוּ֙ אַבְרָהָ֔ם לִ/פְנֵ֖י עַ֥ם הָ/אָֽרֶץ
verses.en: And Avraham bowed before the people of the land.

verses.he: וַ/יְדַבֵּ֨ר אֶל עֶפְר֜וֹן בְּ/אָזְנֵ֤י עַם הָ/אָ֨רֶץ֙ לֵ/אמֹ֔ר אַ֛ךְ אִם אַתָּ֥ה ל֖וּ שְׁמָעֵ֑/נִי נָתַ֜תִּי כֶּ֤סֶף הַ/שָּׂדֶה֙ קַ֣ח מִמֶּ֔/נִּי וְ/אֶקְבְּרָ֥ה אֶת מֵתִ֖/י שָֽׁמָּ/ה
verses.en: And he spoke unto Efron in the hearing of the people of the land, saying: "But if you — only hear me — I give the silver of the field; take it from me, and I shall bury my dead there."

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֧עַן עֶפְר֛וֹן אֶת אַבְרָהָ֖ם לֵ/אמֹ֥ר לֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Efron answered Avraham, saying to him:

verses.he: אֲדֹנִ֣/י שְׁמָעֵ֔/נִי אֶרֶץ֩ אַרְבַּ֨ע מֵאֹ֧ת שֶֽׁקֶל כֶּ֛סֶף בֵּינִ֥/י וּ/בֵֽינְ/ךָ֖ מַה הִ֑וא וְ/אֶת מֵתְ/ךָ֖ קְבֹֽר
verses.en: "My lord, hear me: land of four hundred shekels of silver — between me and you, what is it? Bury your dead."

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁמַ֣ע אַבְרָהָם֮ אֶל עֶפְרוֹן֒ וַ/יִּשְׁקֹ֤ל אַבְרָהָם֙ לְ/עֶפְרֹ֔ן אֶת הַ/כֶּ֕סֶף אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבֶּ֖ר בְּ/אָזְנֵ֣י בְנֵי חֵ֑ת אַרְבַּ֤ע מֵאוֹת֙ שֶׁ֣קֶל כֶּ֔סֶף עֹבֵ֖ר לַ/סֹּחֵֽר
verses.en: And Avraham hearkened unto Efron; and Avraham weighed out to Efron the silver which he had spoken of in the hearing of the sons of Chet: four hundred shekels of silver, passing for the merchant.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֣קָם שְׂדֵ֣ה עֶפְר֗וֹן אֲשֶׁר֙ בַּ/מַּכְפֵּלָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֖ר לִ/פְנֵ֣י מַמְרֵ֑א הַ/שָּׂדֶה֙ וְ/הַ/מְּעָרָ֣ה אֲשֶׁר בּ֔/וֹ וְ/כָל הָ/עֵץ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בַּ/שָּׂדֶ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּ/כָל גְּבֻל֖/וֹ סָבִֽיב
verses.en: And the field of Efron which was in Makhpelah, which was before Mamre — the field and the cave which was in it, and all the trees that were in the field, that were in all its borders round about — was established

verses.he: לְ/אַבְרָהָ֥ם לְ/מִקְנָ֖ה לְ/עֵינֵ֣י בְנֵי חֵ֑ת בְּ/כֹ֖ל בָּאֵ֥י שַֽׁעַר עִירֽ/וֹ
verses.en: to Avraham as a purchase, in the eyes of the sons of Chet, before all who came through the gate of his city.

verses.he: וְ/אַחֲרֵי כֵן֩ קָבַ֨ר אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֶת שָׂרָ֣ה אִשְׁתּ֗/וֹ אֶל מְעָרַ֞ת שְׂדֵ֧ה הַ/מַּכְפֵּלָ֛ה עַל פְּנֵ֥י מַמְרֵ֖א הִ֣וא חֶבְר֑וֹן בְּ/אֶ֖רֶץ כְּנָֽעַן
verses.en: And after this, Avraham buried Sarah his woman in the cave of the field of Makhpelah, facing Mamre — which is Chevron — in the land of Kena'an.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֨קָם הַ/שָּׂדֶ֜ה וְ/הַ/מְּעָרָ֧ה אֲשֶׁר בּ֛/וֹ לְ/אַבְרָהָ֖ם לַ/אֲחֻזַּת קָ֑בֶר מֵ/אֵ֖ת בְּנֵי חֵֽת
verses.en: And the field and the cave which was in it were established to Avraham as a holding of burial, from the sons of Chet.

verses.he: וְ/אַבְרָהָ֣ם זָקֵ֔ן בָּ֖א בַּ/יָּמִ֑ים וַֽ/יהוָ֛ה בֵּרַ֥ךְ אֶת אַבְרָהָ֖ם בַּ/כֹּֽל
verses.en: And Avraham was old, advanced in days; and the LORD had blessed Avraham in all things.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אַבְרָהָ֗ם אֶל עַבְדּ/וֹ֙ זְקַ֣ן בֵּית֔/וֹ הַ/מֹּשֵׁ֖ל בְּ/כָל אֲשֶׁר ל֑/וֹ שִֽׂים נָ֥א יָדְ/ךָ֖ תַּ֥חַת יְרֵכִֽ/י
verses.en: And Avraham said unto his servant, the elder of his house, who ruled over all that was his: "Place, I pray, your hand beneath my thigh.

verses.he: וְ/אַשְׁבִּ֣יעֲ/ךָ֔ בַּֽ/יהוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י הַ/שָּׁמַ֔יִם וֵֽ/אלֹהֵ֖י הָ/אָ֑רֶץ אֲשֶׁ֨ר לֹֽא תִקַּ֤ח אִשָּׁה֙ לִ/בְנִ֔/י מִ/בְּנוֹת֙ הַֽ/כְּנַעֲנִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָנֹכִ֖י יוֹשֵׁ֥ב בְּ/קִרְבּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And I will make you swear by the LORD, God of the heavens and God of the earth, that you shall not take a woman for my son from the daughters of the Kena'ani, among whom I dwell;

verses.he: כִּ֧י אֶל אַרְצִ֛/י וְ/אֶל מוֹלַדְתִּ֖/י תֵּלֵ֑ךְ וְ/לָקַחְתָּ֥ אִשָּׁ֖ה לִ/בְנִ֥/י לְ/יִצְחָֽק
verses.en: but to my land and to my birthplace you shall go, and take a woman for my son, for Yitschak."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָי/ו֙ הָ/עֶ֔בֶד אוּלַי֙ לֹא תֹאבֶ֣ה הָֽ/אִשָּׁ֔ה לָ/לֶ֥כֶת אַחֲרַ֖/י אֶל הָ/אָ֣רֶץ הַ/זֹּ֑את הֶֽ/הָשֵׁ֤ב אָשִׁיב֙ אֶת בִּנְ/ךָ֔ אֶל הָ/אָ֖רֶץ אֲשֶׁר יָצָ֥אתָ מִ/שָּֽׁם
verses.en: And the servant said to him: "Perhaps the woman will not be willing to go after me unto this land. Shall I bring, yes, bring your son back to the land from which you came?"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֵלָ֖י/ו אַבְרָהָ֑ם הִשָּׁ֣מֶר לְ/ךָ֔ פֶּן תָּשִׁ֥יב אֶת בְּנִ֖/י שָֽׁמָּ/ה
verses.en: And Avraham said to him: "Guard yourself, lest you bring my son back there.

verses.he: יְהוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵ֣י הַ/שָּׁמַ֗יִם אֲשֶׁ֨ר לְקָחַ֜/נִי מִ/בֵּ֣ית אָבִ/י֮ וּ/מֵ/אֶ֣רֶץ מֽוֹלַדְתִּ/י֒ וַ/אֲשֶׁ֨ר דִּבֶּר לִ֜/י וַ/אֲשֶׁ֤ר נִֽשְׁבַּֽע לִ/י֙ לֵ/אמֹ֔ר לְ/זַ֨רְעֲ/ךָ֔ אֶתֵּ֖ן אֶת הָ/אָ֣רֶץ הַ/זֹּ֑את ה֗וּא יִשְׁלַ֤ח מַלְאָכ/וֹ֙ לְ/פָנֶ֔י/ךָ וְ/לָקַחְתָּ֥ אִשָּׁ֛ה לִ/בְנִ֖/י מִ/שָּֽׁם
verses.en: The LORD, God of the heavens, who took me from the house of my father and from the land of my birth, and who spoke to me and who swore to me, saying, 'To your seed I shall give this land' — He shall send His messenger before you, and you shall take a woman for my son from there.

verses.he: וְ/אִם לֹ֨א תֹאבֶ֤ה הָֽ/אִשָּׁה֙ לָ/לֶ֣כֶת אַחֲרֶ֔י/ךָ וְ/נִקִּ֕יתָ מִ/שְּׁבֻעָתִ֖/י זֹ֑את רַ֣ק אֶת בְּנִ֔/י לֹ֥א תָשֵׁ֖ב שָֽׁמָּ/ה
verses.en: And if the woman is not willing to go after you, then you shall be clear of this oath of mine. Only my son you shall not bring back there."

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֤שֶׂם הָ/עֶ֨בֶד֙ אֶת יָד֔/וֹ תַּ֛חַת יֶ֥רֶךְ אַבְרָהָ֖ם אֲדֹנָ֑י/ו וַ/יִּשָּׁ֣בַֽע ל֔/וֹ עַל הַ/דָּבָ֖ר הַ/זֶּֽה
verses.en: And the servant placed his hand beneath the thigh of Avraham his master, and swore to him concerning this matter.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקַּ֣ח הָ֠/עֶבֶד עֲשָׂרָ֨ה גְמַלִּ֜ים מִ/גְּמַלֵּ֤י אֲדֹנָי/ו֙ וַ/יֵּ֔לֶךְ וְ/כָל ט֥וּב אֲדֹנָ֖י/ו בְּ/יָד֑/וֹ וַ/יָּ֗קָם וַ/יֵּ֛לֶךְ אֶל אֲרַ֥ם נַֽהֲרַ֖יִם אֶל עִ֥יר נָחֽוֹר
verses.en: And the servant took ten camels from the camels of his master and went, with all the good of his master in his hand; and he rose and went to Aram of the Two Rivers, to the city of Nachor.

verses.he: וַ/יַּבְרֵ֧ךְ הַ/גְּמַלִּ֛ים מִ/ח֥וּץ לָ/עִ֖יר אֶל בְּאֵ֣ר הַ/מָּ֑יִם לְ/עֵ֣ת עֶ֔רֶב לְ/עֵ֖ת צֵ֥את הַ/שֹּׁאֲבֹֽת
verses.en: And he made the camels kneel outside the city, by the well of water, at the time of evening, at the time when the women who draw water go out.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמַ֓ר יְהוָ֗ה אֱלֹהֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם הַקְרֵה נָ֥א לְ/פָנַ֖/י הַ/יּ֑וֹם וַ/עֲשֵׂה חֶ֕סֶד עִ֖ם אֲדֹנִ֥/י אַבְרָהָֽם
verses.en: And he said: "O LORD, God of my master Avraham, let it happen, I pray, before me today, and do loyal-kindness with my master Avraham.

verses.he: הִנֵּ֛ה אָנֹכִ֥י נִצָּ֖ב עַל עֵ֣ין הַ/מָּ֑יִם וּ/בְנוֹת֙ אַנְשֵׁ֣י הָ/עִ֔יר יֹצְאֹ֖ת לִ/שְׁאֹ֥ב מָֽיִם
verses.en: Behold, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.

verses.he: וְ/הָיָ֣ה הַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֗ אֲשֶׁ֨ר אֹמַ֤ר אֵלֶ֨י/הָ֙ הַטִּי נָ֤א כַדֵּ/ךְ֙ וְ/אֶשְׁתֶּ֔ה וְ/אָמְרָ֣ה שְׁתֵ֔ה וְ/גַם גְּמַלֶּ֖י/ךָ אַשְׁקֶ֑ה אֹתָ֤/הּ הֹכַ֨חְתָּ֙ לְ/עַבְדְּ/ךָ֣ לְ/יִצְחָ֔ק וּ/בָ֣/הּ אֵדַ֔ע כִּי עָשִׂ֥יתָ חֶ֖סֶד עִם אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And let it be that the young woman to whom I shall say, 'Incline, I pray, your jar that I may drink,' and she says, 'Drink, and also your camels I shall water' — her You have appointed for Your servant, for Yitschak; and by her I shall know that You have done loyal-kindness with my master."

verses.he: וַֽ/יְהִי ה֗וּא טֶרֶם֮ כִּלָּ֣ה לְ/דַבֵּר֒ וְ/הִנֵּ֧ה רִבְקָ֣ה יֹצֵ֗את אֲשֶׁ֤ר יֻלְּדָה֙ לִ/בְתוּאֵ֣ל בֶּן מִלְכָּ֔ה אֵ֥שֶׁת נָח֖וֹר אֲחִ֣י אַבְרָהָ֑ם וְ/כַדָּ֖/הּ עַל שִׁכְמָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And it was: before he had finished speaking — and behold, Rivqah was coming out, who was born to Betu'el son of Milkah, the woman of Nachor, brother of Avraham; and her jar was upon her shoulder.

verses.he: וְ/הַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֗ טֹבַ֤ת מַרְאֶה֙ מְאֹ֔ד בְּתוּלָ֕ה וְ/אִ֖ישׁ לֹ֣א יְדָעָ֑/הּ וַ/תֵּ֣רֶד הָ/עַ֔יְנָ/ה וַ/תְּמַלֵּ֥א כַדָּ֖/הּ וַ/תָּֽעַל
verses.en: And the young woman was very good of appearance, a maiden, and no man had known her; and she went down to the spring and filled her jar and came up.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֥רָץ הָ/עֶ֖בֶד לִ/קְרָאתָ֑/הּ וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר הַגְמִיאִ֥י/נִי נָ֛א מְעַט מַ֖יִם מִ/כַּדֵּֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And the servant ran to meet her and said: "Let me sip, I pray, a little water from your jar."

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֖אמֶר שְׁתֵ֣ה אֲדֹנִ֑/י וַ/תְּמַהֵ֗ר וַ/תֹּ֧רֶד כַּדָּ֛/הּ עַל יָדָ֖/הּ וַ/תַּשְׁקֵֽ/הוּ
verses.en: And she said: "Drink, my lord." And she hastened and lowered her jar upon her hand and gave him drink.

verses.he: וַ/תְּכַ֖ל לְ/הַשְׁקֹת֑/וֹ וַ/תֹּ֗אמֶר גַּ֤ם לִ/גְמַלֶּ֨י/ךָ֙ אֶשְׁאָ֔ב עַ֥ד אִם כִּלּ֖וּ לִ/שְׁתֹּֽת
verses.en: And she finished giving him drink and said: "Also for your camels I shall draw, until they have finished drinking."

verses.he: וַ/תְּמַהֵ֗ר וַ/תְּעַ֤ר כַּדָּ/הּ֙ אֶל הַ/שֹּׁ֔קֶת וַ/תָּ֥רָץ ע֛וֹד אֶֽל הַ/בְּאֵ֖ר לִ/שְׁאֹ֑ב וַ/תִּשְׁאַ֖ב לְ/כָל גְּמַלָּֽי/ו
verses.en: And she hastened and emptied her jar into the trough and ran again to the well to draw, and drew for all his camels.

verses.he: וְ/הָ/אִ֥ישׁ מִשְׁתָּאֵ֖ה לָ֑/הּ מַחֲרִ֕ישׁ לָ/דַ֗עַת הַֽ/הִצְלִ֧יחַ יְהוָ֛ה דַּרְכּ֖/וֹ אִם לֹֽא
verses.en: And the man was gazing at her in silence, waiting to know whether the LORD had made his way prosper or not.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֨ר כִּלּ֤וּ הַ/גְּמַלִּים֙ לִ/שְׁתּ֔וֹת וַ/יִּקַּ֤ח הָ/אִישׁ֙ נֶ֣זֶם זָהָ֔ב בֶּ֖קַע מִשְׁקָל֑/וֹ וּ/שְׁנֵ֤י צְמִידִים֙ עַל יָדֶ֔י/הָ עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מִשְׁקָלָֽ/ם
verses.en: And it was, when the camels had finished drinking, that the man took a golden ring, a half-shekel its weight, and two bracelets for her hands, ten of gold their weight.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ בַּת מִ֣י אַ֔תְּ הַגִּ֥ידִי נָ֖א לִ֑/י הֲ/יֵ֧שׁ בֵּית אָבִ֛י/ךְ מָק֥וֹם לָ֖/נוּ לָ/לִֽין
verses.en: And he said: "Whose daughter are you? Tell me, I pray: is there room in the house of your father for us to lodge?"

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֔י/ו בַּת בְּתוּאֵ֖ל אָנֹ֑כִי בֶּן מִלְכָּ֕ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָלְדָ֖ה לְ/נָחֽוֹר
verses.en: And she said to him: "I am the daughter of Betu'el, son of Milkah, whom she bore to Nachor."

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֔י/ו גַּם תֶּ֥בֶן גַּם מִסְפּ֖וֹא רַ֣ב עִמָּ֑/נוּ גַּם מָק֖וֹם לָ/לֽוּן
verses.en: And she said to him: "Both straw and fodder are plentiful with us, and also a place to lodge."

verses.he: וַ/יִּקֹּ֣ד הָ/אִ֔ישׁ וַ/יִּשְׁתַּ֖חוּ לַֽ/יהוָֽה
verses.en: And the man bowed his head and bowed down to the LORD.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר בָּר֤וּךְ יְהוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם אֲ֠שֶׁר לֹֽא עָזַ֥ב חַסְדּ֛/וֹ וַ/אֲמִתּ֖/וֹ מֵ/עִ֣ם אֲדֹנִ֑/י אָנֹכִ֗י בַּ/דֶּ֨רֶךְ֙ נָחַ֣/נִי יְהוָ֔ה בֵּ֖ית אֲחֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said: "Blessed be the LORD, God of my master Avraham, who has not forsaken His loyal-kindness and His faithfulness from my master. As for me — on the way the LORD has led me to the house of my master's brothers."
And the young woman ran and told her mother's household these things.

verses.he: וּ/לְ/רִבְקָ֥ה אָ֖ח וּ/שְׁמ֣/וֹ לָבָ֑ן וַ/יָּ֨רָץ לָבָ֧ן אֶל הָ/אִ֛ישׁ הַ/ח֖וּצָ/ה אֶל הָ/עָֽיִן
verses.en: And Rivqah had a brother, and his name was Lavan; and Lavan ran out unto the man, to the spring.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֣י כִּ/רְאֹ֣ת אֶת הַ/נֶּ֗זֶם וְֽ/אֶת הַ/צְּמִדִים֮ עַל יְדֵ֣י אֲחֹת/וֹ֒ וּ/כְ/שָׁמְע֗/וֹ אֶת דִּבְרֵ֞י רִבְקָ֤ה אֲחֹת/וֹ֙ לֵ/אמֹ֔ר כֹּֽה דִבֶּ֥ר אֵלַ֖/י הָ/אִ֑ישׁ וַ/יָּבֹא֙ אֶל הָ/אִ֔ישׁ וְ/הִנֵּ֛ה עֹמֵ֥ד עַל הַ/גְּמַלִּ֖ים עַל הָ/עָֽיִן
verses.en: And it was, when he saw the ring and the bracelets upon the hands of his sister, and when he heard the words of Rivqah his sister, saying, "Thus the man spoke to me" — that he came unto the man; and behold, he was standing by the camels at the spring.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר בּ֖וֹא בְּר֣וּךְ יְהוָ֑ה לָ֤/מָּה תַעֲמֹד֙ בַּ/ח֔וּץ וְ/אָנֹכִי֙ פִּנִּ֣יתִי הַ/בַּ֔יִת וּ/מָק֖וֹם לַ/גְּמַלִּֽים
verses.en: And he said: "Come, blessed of the LORD! Why do you stand outside? For I have cleared the house, and a place for the camels."

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֤א הָ/אִישׁ֙ הַ/בַּ֔יְתָ/ה וַ/יְפַתַּ֖ח הַ/גְּמַלִּ֑ים וַ/יִּתֵּ֨ן תֶּ֤בֶן וּ/מִסְפּוֹא֙ לַ/גְּמַלִּ֔ים וּ/מַ֨יִם֙ לִ/רְחֹ֣ץ רַגְלָ֔י/ו וְ/רַגְלֵ֥י הָ/אֲנָשִׁ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר אִתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And the man came into the house; and he unloaded the camels and gave straw and fodder to the camels, and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.

verses.he: ו/יישם וַ/יּוּשַׂ֤ם לְ/פָנָי/ו֙ לֶ/אֱכֹ֔ל וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ לֹ֣א אֹכַ֔ל עַ֥ד אִם דִּבַּ֖רְתִּי דְּבָרָ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר דַּבֵּֽר
verses.en: And food was set before him to eat; but he said: "I shall not eat until I have spoken my words." And he said: "Speak."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמַ֑ר עֶ֥בֶד אַבְרָהָ֖ם אָנֹֽכִי
verses.en: And he said: "I am the servant of Avraham.

verses.he: וַ/יהוָ֞ה בֵּרַ֧ךְ אֶת אֲדֹנִ֛/י מְאֹ֖ד וַ/יִּגְדָּ֑ל וַ/יִּתֶּן ל֞/וֹ צֹ֤אן וּ/בָקָר֙ וְ/כֶ֣סֶף וְ/זָהָ֔ב וַ/עֲבָדִם֙ וּ/שְׁפָחֹ֔ת וּ/גְמַלִּ֖ים וַ/חֲמֹרִֽים
verses.en: And the LORD has blessed my master greatly, and he has become great; and He has given him flocks and herds, and silver and gold, and servants and maidservants, and camels and donkeys.

verses.he: וַ/תֵּ֡לֶד שָׂרָה֩ אֵ֨שֶׁת אֲדֹנִ֥/י בֵן֙ לַֽ/אדֹנִ֔/י אַחֲרֵ֖י זִקְנָתָ֑/הּ וַ/יִּתֶּן לּ֖/וֹ אֶת כָּל אֲשֶׁר לֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Sarah, the woman of my master, bore a son to my master after her old age; and he has given him all that is his.

verses.he: וַ/יַּשְׁבִּעֵ֥/נִי אֲדֹנִ֖/י לֵ/אמֹ֑ר לֹא תִקַּ֤ח אִשָּׁה֙ לִ/בְנִ֔/י מִ/בְּנוֹת֙ הַֽ/כְּנַעֲנִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָנֹכִ֖י יֹשֵׁ֥ב בְּ/אַרְצֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And my master made me swear, saying: 'You shall not take a woman for my son from the daughters of the Kena'ani, in whose land I dwell.

verses.he: אִם לֹ֧א אֶל בֵּית אָבִ֛/י תֵּלֵ֖ךְ וְ/אֶל מִשְׁפַּחְתִּ֑/י וְ/לָקַחְתָּ֥ אִשָּׁ֖ה לִ/בְנִֽ/י
verses.en: Rather, to the house of my father you shall go, and to my family, and take a woman for my son.'

verses.he: וָ/אֹמַ֖ר אֶל אֲדֹנִ֑/י אֻלַ֛י לֹא תֵלֵ֥ךְ הָ/אִשָּׁ֖ה אַחֲרָֽ/י
verses.en: And I said to my master: 'Perhaps the woman will not go after me.'

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אֵלָ֑/י יְהוָ֞ה אֲשֶׁר הִתְהַלַּ֣כְתִּי לְ/פָנָ֗י/ו יִשְׁלַ֨ח מַלְאָכ֤/וֹ אִתָּ/ךְ֙ וְ/הִצְלִ֣יחַ דַּרְכֶּ֔/ךָ וְ/לָקַחְתָּ֤ אִשָּׁה֙ לִ/בְנִ֔/י מִ/מִּשְׁפַּחְתִּ֖/י וּ/מִ/בֵּ֥ית אָבִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said to me: 'The LORD, before whom I have walked, shall send His messenger with you and make your way prosper; and you shall take a woman for my son from my family and from the house of my father.

verses.he: אָ֤ז תִּנָּקֶה֙ מֵ/אָ֣לָתִ֔/י כִּ֥י תָב֖וֹא אֶל מִשְׁפַּחְתִּ֑/י וְ/אִם לֹ֤א יִתְּנוּ֙ לָ֔/ךְ וְ/הָיִ֥יתָ נָקִ֖י מֵ/אָלָתִֽ/י
verses.en: Then you shall be clear of my oath: when you come to my family, and if they do not give her to you, then you shall be clear of my oath.'

verses.he: וָ/אָבֹ֥א הַ/יּ֖וֹם אֶל הָ/עָ֑יִן וָ/אֹמַ֗ר יְהוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם אִם יֶשְׁ/ךָ נָּא֙ מַצְלִ֣יחַ דַּרְכִּ֔/י אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָנֹכִ֖י הֹלֵ֥ךְ עָלֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And I came today to the spring and said: 'O LORD, God of my master Avraham, if You are making my way prosper, upon which I walk —

verses.he: הִנֵּ֛ה אָנֹכִ֥י נִצָּ֖ב עַל עֵ֣ין הַ/מָּ֑יִם וְ/הָיָ֤ה הָֽ/עַלְמָה֙ הַ/יֹּצֵ֣את לִ/שְׁאֹ֔ב וְ/אָמַרְתִּ֣י אֵלֶ֔י/הָ הַשְׁקִֽי/נִי נָ֥א מְעַט מַ֖יִם מִ/כַּדֵּֽ/ךְ
verses.en: behold, I am standing by the spring of water; and let it be that the maiden who comes out to draw, and I say to her, "Give me, I pray, a little water to drink from your jar,"

verses.he: וְ/אָמְרָ֤ה אֵלַ/י֙ גַּם אַתָּ֣ה שְׁתֵ֔ה וְ/גַ֥ם לִ/גְמַלֶּ֖י/ךָ אֶשְׁאָ֑ב הִ֣וא הָֽ/אִשָּׁ֔ה אֲשֶׁר הֹכִ֥יחַ יְהוָ֖ה לְ/בֶן אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: and she says to me, "Both you drink, and also for your camels I shall draw" — she is the woman whom the LORD has appointed for the son of my master.'

verses.he: אֲנִי֩ טֶ֨רֶם אֲכַלֶּ֜ה לְ/דַבֵּ֣ר אֶל לִבִּ֗/י וְ/הִנֵּ֨ה רִבְקָ֤ה יֹצֵאת֙ וְ/כַדָּ֣/הּ עַל שִׁכְמָ֔/הּ וַ/תֵּ֥רֶד הָ/עַ֖יְנָ/ה וַ/תִּשְׁאָ֑ב וָ/אֹמַ֥ר אֵלֶ֖י/הָ הַשְׁקִ֥י/נִי נָֽא
verses.en: Before I had finished speaking to my heart — and behold, Rivqah was coming out, and her jar upon her shoulder; and she went down to the spring and drew. And I said to her: 'Give me drink, I pray.'

verses.he: וַ/תְּמַהֵ֗ר וַ/תּ֤וֹרֶד כַּדָּ/הּ֙ מֵֽ/עָלֶ֔י/הָ וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר שְׁתֵ֔ה וְ/גַם גְּמַלֶּ֖י/ךָ אַשְׁקֶ֑ה וָ/אֵ֕שְׁתְּ וְ/גַ֥ם הַ/גְּמַלִּ֖ים הִשְׁקָֽתָה
verses.en: And she hastened and lowered her jar from upon her and said: 'Drink, and also your camels I shall water.' And I drank, and also the camels she watered.

verses.he: וָ/אֶשְׁאַ֣ל אֹתָ֗/הּ וָ/אֹמַר֮ בַּת מִ֣י אַתְּ֒ וַ/תֹּ֗אמֶר בַּת בְּתוּאֵל֙ בֶּן נָח֔וֹר אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָֽלְדָה לּ֖/וֹ מִלְכָּ֑ה וָ/אָשִׂ֤ם הַ/נֶּ֨זֶם֙ עַל אַפָּ֔/הּ וְ/הַ/צְּמִידִ֖ים עַל יָדֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And I asked her and said: 'Whose daughter are you?' And she said: 'The daughter of Betu'el, son of Nachor, whom Milkah bore to him.' And I placed the ring upon her nose and the bracelets upon her hands.

verses.he: וָ/אֶקֹּ֥ד וָֽ/אֶשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֖ה לַ/יהוָ֑ה וָ/אֲבָרֵ֗ךְ אֶת יְהוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם אֲשֶׁ֤ר הִנְחַ֨/נִי֙ בְּ/דֶ֣רֶךְ אֱמֶ֔ת לָ/קַ֛חַת אֶת בַּת אֲחִ֥י אֲדֹנִ֖/י לִ/בְנֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And I bowed my head and bowed down to the LORD, and I blessed the LORD, God of my master Avraham, who had led me on the way of truth to take the daughter of the brother of my master for his son.

verses.he: וְ֠/עַתָּה אִם יֶשְׁ/כֶ֨ם עֹשִׂ֜ים חֶ֧סֶד וֶֽ/אֱמֶ֛ת אֶת אֲדֹנִ֖/י הַגִּ֣ידוּ לִ֑/י וְ/אִם לֹ֕א הַגִּ֣ידוּ לִ֔/י וְ/אֶפְנֶ֥ה עַל יָמִ֖ין א֥וֹ עַל שְׂמֹֽאל
verses.en: And now, if you will do loyal-kindness and faithfulness with my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, that I may turn to the right or to the left."

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֨עַן לָבָ֤ן וּ/בְתוּאֵל֙ וַ/יֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ מֵ/יְהוָ֖ה יָצָ֣א הַ/דָּבָ֑ר לֹ֥א נוּכַ֛ל דַּבֵּ֥ר אֵלֶ֖י/ךָ רַ֥ע אוֹ טֽוֹב
verses.en: And Lavan and Betu'el answered and said: "From the LORD the thing has gone forth; we are not able to speak to you evil or good.

verses.he: הִנֵּֽה רִבְקָ֥ה לְ/פָנֶ֖י/ךָ קַ֣ח וָ/לֵ֑ךְ וּ/תְהִ֤י אִשָּׁה֙ לְ/בֶן אֲדֹנֶ֔י/ךָ כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֖ר דִּבֶּ֥ר יְהוָֽה
verses.en: Behold, Rivqah is before you; take her and go, and let her be a woman for the son of your master, as the LORD has spoken."

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֕י כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁמַ֛ע עֶ֥בֶד אַבְרָהָ֖ם אֶת דִּבְרֵי/הֶ֑ם וַ/יִּשְׁתַּ֥חוּ אַ֖רְצָ/ה לַֽ/יהוָֽה
verses.en: And it was, when the servant of Avraham heard their words, that he bowed down to the earth before the LORD.

verses.he: וַ/יּוֹצֵ֨א הָ/עֶ֜בֶד כְּלֵי כֶ֨סֶף וּ/כְלֵ֤י זָהָב֙ וּ/בְגָדִ֔ים וַ/יִּתֵּ֖ן לְ/רִבְקָ֑ה וּ/מִ֨גְדָּנֹ֔ת נָתַ֥ן לְ/אָחִ֖י/הָ וּ/לְ/אִמָּֽ/הּ
verses.en: And the servant brought out vessels of silver and vessels of gold and garments, and gave them to Rivqah; and precious gifts he gave to her brother and to her mother.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאכְל֣וּ וַ/יִּשְׁתּ֗וּ ה֛וּא וְ/הָ/אֲנָשִׁ֥ים אֲשֶׁר עִמּ֖/וֹ וַ/יָּלִ֑ינוּ וַ/יָּק֣וּמוּ בַ/בֹּ֔קֶר וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר שַׁלְּחֻ֥/נִי לַֽ/אדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And they ate and drank, he and the men who were with him, and they lodged. And they rose in the morning, and he said: "Send me to my master."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אָחִ֨י/הָ֙ וְ/אִמָּ֔/הּ תֵּשֵׁ֨ב הַ/נַּעֲרָ֥ אִתָּ֛/נוּ יָמִ֖ים א֣וֹ עָשׂ֑וֹר אַחַ֖ר תֵּלֵֽךְ
verses.en: And her brother and her mother said: "Let the young woman stay with us some days, or ten; after that she shall go."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֲלֵ/הֶם֙ אַל תְּאַחֲר֣וּ אֹתִ֔/י וַֽ/יהוָ֖ה הִצְלִ֣יחַ דַּרְכִּ֑/י שַׁלְּח֕וּ/נִי וְ/אֵלְכָ֖ה לַֽ/אדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said to them: "Do not delay me, for the LORD has made my way prosper. Send me away, that I may go to my master."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמְר֖וּ נִקְרָ֣א לַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֑ וְ/נִשְׁאֲלָ֖ה אֶת פִּֽי/הָ
verses.en: And they said: "Let us call the young woman and ask her own mouth."

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרְא֤וּ לְ/רִבְקָה֙ וַ/יֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלֶ֔י/הָ הֲ/תֵלְכִ֖י עִם הָ/אִ֣ישׁ הַ/זֶּ֑ה וַ/תֹּ֖אמֶר אֵלֵֽךְ
verses.en: And they called Rivqah and said to her: "Will you go with this man?" And she said: "I will go."

verses.he: וַֽ/יְשַׁלְּח֛וּ אֶת רִבְקָ֥ה אֲחֹתָ֖/ם וְ/אֶת מֵנִקְתָּ֑/הּ וְ/אֶת עֶ֥בֶד אַבְרָהָ֖ם וְ/אֶת אֲנָשָֽׁי/ו
verses.en: And they sent away Rivqah their sister and her nurse, and the servant of Avraham and his men.

verses.he: וַ/יְבָרֲכ֤וּ אֶת רִבְקָה֙ וַ/יֹּ֣אמְרוּ לָ֔/הּ אֲחֹתֵ֕/נוּ אַ֥תְּ הֲיִ֖י לְ/אַלְפֵ֣י רְבָבָ֑ה וְ/יִירַ֣שׁ זַרְעֵ֔/ךְ אֵ֖ת שַׁ֥עַר שֹׂנְאָֽי/ו
verses.en: And they blessed Rivqah and said to her: "Our sister, may you become thousands of ten-thousands; and may your seed possess the gate of those who hate them."

verses.he: וַ/תָּ֨קָם רִבְקָ֜ה וְ/נַעֲרֹתֶ֗י/הָ וַ/תִּרְכַּ֨בְנָה֙ עַל הַ/גְּמַלִּ֔ים וַ/תֵּלַ֖כְנָה אַחֲרֵ֣י הָ/אִ֑ישׁ וַ/יִּקַּ֥ח הָ/עֶ֛בֶד אֶת רִבְקָ֖ה וַ/יֵּלַֽךְ
verses.en: And Rivqah rose, and her young women, and they rode upon the camels and went after the man; and the servant took Rivqah and went.

verses.he: וְ/יִצְחָק֙ בָּ֣א מִ/בּ֔וֹא בְּאֵ֥ר לַחַ֖י רֹאִ֑י וְ/ה֥וּא יוֹשֵׁ֖ב בְּ/אֶ֥רֶץ הַ/נֶּֽגֶב
verses.en: And Yitschak had come from the way of Be'er-Lachai-Ro'i, for he was dwelling in the land of the Negev.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּצֵ֥א יִצְחָ֛ק לָ/שׂ֥וּחַ בַּ/שָּׂדֶ֖ה לִ/פְנ֣וֹת עָ֑רֶב וַ/יִּשָּׂ֤א עֵינָי/ו֙ וַ/יַּ֔רְא וְ/הִנֵּ֥ה גְמַלִּ֖ים בָּאִֽים
verses.en: And Yitschak went out to meditate in the field toward the turning of evening; and he lifted up his eyes and saw — and behold, camels coming.

verses.he: וַ/תִּשָּׂ֤א רִבְקָה֙ אֶת עֵינֶ֔י/הָ וַ/תֵּ֖רֶא אֶת יִצְחָ֑ק וַ/תִּפֹּ֖ל מֵ/עַ֥ל הַ/גָּמָֽל
verses.en: And Rivqah lifted up her eyes and saw Yitschak, and she fell from upon the camel.

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֶל הָ/עֶ֗בֶד מִֽי הָ/אִ֤ישׁ הַלָּזֶה֙ הַ/הֹלֵ֤ךְ בַּ/שָּׂדֶה֙ לִ/קְרָאתֵ֔/נוּ וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר הָ/עֶ֖בֶד ה֣וּא אֲדֹנִ֑/י וַ/תִּקַּ֥ח הַ/צָּעִ֖יף וַ/תִּתְכָּֽס
verses.en: And she said unto the servant: "Who is this man walking in the field to meet us?" And the servant said: "He is my master." And she took the veil and covered herself.

verses.he: וַ/יְסַפֵּ֥ר הָ/עֶ֖בֶד לְ/יִצְחָ֑ק אֵ֥ת כָּל הַ/דְּבָרִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשָֽׂה
verses.en: And the servant told Yitschak all the things that he had done.

verses.he: וַ/יְבִאֶ֣/הָ יִצְחָ֗ק הָ/אֹ֨הֱלָ/ה֙ שָׂרָ֣ה אִמּ֔/וֹ וַ/יִּקַּ֧ח אֶת רִבְקָ֛ה וַ/תְּהִי ל֥/וֹ לְ/אִשָּׁ֖ה וַ/יֶּאֱהָבֶ֑/הָ וַ/יִּנָּחֵ֥ם יִצְחָ֖ק אַחֲרֵ֥י אִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Yitschak brought her into the tent of Sarah his mother; and he took Rivqah, and she became his woman, and he loved her. And Yitschak was comforted after his mother.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֧סֶף אַבְרָהָ֛ם וַ/יִּקַּ֥ח אִשָּׁ֖ה וּ/שְׁמָ֥/הּ קְטוּרָֽה
verses.en: And Avraham took again a wife, and her name was Keturah.

verses.he: וַ/תֵּ֣לֶד ל֗/וֹ אֶת זִמְרָן֙ וְ/אֶת יָקְשָׁ֔ן וְ/אֶת מְדָ֖ן וְ/אֶת מִדְיָ֑ן וְ/אֶת יִשְׁבָּ֖ק וְ/אֶת שֽׁוּחַ
verses.en: And she bore him Zimran, and Yokshan, and Medan, and Midyan, and Yishbak, and Shuach.

verses.he: וְ/יָקְשָׁ֣ן יָלַ֔ד אֶת שְׁבָ֖א וְ/אֶת דְּדָ֑ן וּ/בְנֵ֣י דְדָ֔ן הָי֛וּ אַשּׁוּרִ֥ם וּ/לְטוּשִׁ֖ים וּ/לְאֻמִּֽים
verses.en: And Yokshan begot Sheva and Dedan. And the sons of Dedan were the Asshurim, and the Letushim, and the Leummim.

verses.he: וּ/בְנֵ֣י מִדְיָ֗ן עֵיפָ֤ה וָ/עֵ֨פֶר֙ וַ/חֲנֹ֔ךְ וַ/אֲבִידָ֖ע וְ/אֶלְדָּעָ֑ה כָּל אֵ֖לֶּה בְּנֵ֥י קְטוּרָֽה
verses.en: And the sons of Midyan: Ephah, and Epher, and Chanokh, and Avida, and Eldaah. All these were the sons of Keturah.

verses.he: וַ/יִּתֵּ֧ן אַבְרָהָ֛ם אֶת כָּל אֲשֶׁר ל֖/וֹ לְ/יִצְחָֽק
verses.en: And Avraham gave all that he had unto Yitschak.

verses.he: וְ/לִ/בְנֵ֤י הַ/פִּֽילַגְשִׁים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְ/אַבְרָהָ֔ם נָתַ֥ן אַבְרָהָ֖ם מַתָּנֹ֑ת וַֽ/יְשַׁלְּחֵ֞/ם מֵ/עַ֨ל יִצְחָ֤ק בְּנ/וֹ֙ בְּ/עוֹדֶ֣/נּוּ חַ֔י קֵ֖דְמָ/ה אֶל אֶ֥רֶץ קֶֽדֶם
verses.en: And to the sons of the concubines that Avraham had, Avraham gave gifts; and he sent them away from Yitschak his son, while he yet lived, eastward, unto the land of the East.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗לֶּה יְמֵ֛י שְׁנֵֽי חַיֵּ֥י אַבְרָהָ֖ם אֲשֶׁר חָ֑י מְאַ֥ת שָׁנָ֛ה וְ/שִׁבְעִ֥ים שָׁנָ֖ה וְ/חָמֵ֥שׁ שָׁנִֽים
verses.en: And these are the days of the years of Avraham's life which he lived: a hundred years and seventy years and five years.

verses.he: וַ/יִּגְוַ֨ע וַ/יָּ֧מָת אַבְרָהָ֛ם בְּ/שֵׂיבָ֥ה טוֹבָ֖ה זָקֵ֣ן וְ/שָׂבֵ֑עַ וַ/יֵּאָ֖סֶף אֶל עַמָּֽי/ו
verses.en: And Avraham breathed his last and died in a good grey age, old and satisfied; and he was gathered unto his people.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְבְּר֨וּ אֹת֜/וֹ יִצְחָ֤ק וְ/יִשְׁמָעֵאל֙ בָּנָ֔י/ו אֶל מְעָרַ֖ת הַ/מַּכְפֵּלָ֑ה אֶל שְׂדֵ֞ה עֶפְרֹ֤ן בֶּן צֹ֨חַר֙ הַֽ/חִתִּ֔י אֲשֶׁ֖ר עַל פְּנֵ֥י מַמְרֵֽא
verses.en: And Yitschak and Yishmael his sons buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron son of Tsochar the Chittite, which faces Mamre —

verses.he: הַ/שָּׂדֶ֛ה אֲשֶׁר קָנָ֥ה אַבְרָהָ֖ם מֵ/אֵ֣ת בְּנֵי חֵ֑ת שָׁ֛מָּ/ה קֻבַּ֥ר אַבְרָהָ֖ם וְ/שָׂרָ֥ה אִשְׁתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: the field which Avraham had purchased from the sons of Chet. There was Avraham buried, and Sarah his wife.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י אַחֲרֵי֙ מ֣וֹת אַבְרָהָ֔ם וַ/יְבָ֥רֶךְ אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶת יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֑/וֹ וַ/יֵּ֣שֶׁב יִצְחָ֔ק עִם בְּאֵ֥ר לַחַ֖י רֹאִֽי
verses.en: And it was, after the death of Avraham, that God blessed Yitschak his son; and Yitschak dwelt by the Well of the Living One Who Sees.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֛לֶּה תֹּלְדֹ֥ת יִשְׁמָעֵ֖אל בֶּן אַבְרָהָ֑ם אֲשֶׁ֨ר יָלְדָ֜ה הָגָ֧ר הַ/מִּצְרִ֛ית שִׁפְחַ֥ת שָׂרָ֖ה לְ/אַבְרָהָֽם
verses.en: And these are the begettings of Yishmael son of Avraham, whom Hagar the Mitsrite, handmaid of Sarah, bore unto Avraham.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗לֶּה שְׁמוֹת֙ בְּנֵ֣י יִשְׁמָעֵ֔אל בִּ/שְׁמֹתָ֖/ם לְ/תוֹלְדֹתָ֑/ם בְּכֹ֤ר יִשְׁמָעֵאל֙ נְבָיֹ֔ת וְ/קֵדָ֥ר וְ/אַדְבְּאֵ֖ל וּ/מִבְשָֽׂם
verses.en: And these are the names of the sons of Yishmael, by their names, according to their begettings: the firstborn of Yishmael, Nevayot; and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mivsam,

verses.he: וּ/מִשְׁמָ֥ע וְ/דוּמָ֖ה וּ/מַשָּֽׂא
verses.en: and Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,

verses.he: חֲדַ֣ד וְ/תֵימָ֔א יְט֥וּר נָפִ֖ישׁ וָ/קֵֽדְמָה
verses.en: Chadad, and Tema, Yetur, Naphish, and Kedmah.

verses.he: אֵ֣לֶּה הֵ֞ם בְּנֵ֤י יִשְׁמָעֵאל֙ וְ/אֵ֣לֶּה שְׁמֹתָ֔/ם בְּ/חַצְרֵי/הֶ֖ם וּ/בְ/טִֽירֹתָ֑/ם שְׁנֵים עָשָׂ֥ר נְשִׂיאִ֖ם לְ/אֻמֹּתָֽ/ם
verses.en: These are the sons of Yishmael, and these are their names, by their enclosures and by their strongholds — twelve princes according to their peoples.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗לֶּה שְׁנֵי֙ חַיֵּ֣י יִשְׁמָעֵ֔אל מְאַ֥ת שָׁנָ֛ה וּ/שְׁלֹשִׁ֥ים שָׁנָ֖ה וְ/שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֑ים וַ/יִּגְוַ֣ע וַ/יָּ֔מָת וַ/יֵּאָ֖סֶף אֶל עַמָּֽי/ו
verses.en: And these are the years of the life of Yishmael: a hundred years and thirty years and seven years; and he breathed his last and died, and was gathered unto his people.

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁכְּנ֨וּ מֵֽ/חֲוִילָ֜ה עַד שׁ֗וּר אֲשֶׁר֙ עַל פְּנֵ֣י מִצְרַ֔יִם בֹּאֲ/כָ֖ה אַשּׁ֑וּרָ/ה עַל פְּנֵ֥י כָל אֶחָ֖י/ו נָפָֽל
verses.en: And they dwelt from Chavilah unto Shur, which faces Mitsrayim, as you go toward Asshur. Before the face of all his brothers he fell.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֛לֶּה תּוֹלְדֹ֥ת יִצְחָ֖ק בֶּן אַבְרָהָ֑ם אַבְרָהָ֖ם הוֹלִ֥יד אֶת יִצְחָֽק
verses.en: And these are the begettings of Yitschak son of Avraham: Avraham begot Yitschak.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֤י יִצְחָק֙ בֶּן אַרְבָּעִ֣ים שָׁנָ֔ה בְּ/קַחְתּ֣/וֹ אֶת רִבְקָ֗ה בַּת בְּתוּאֵל֙ הָֽ/אֲרַמִּ֔י מִ/פַּדַּ֖ן אֲרָ֑ם אֲח֛וֹת לָבָ֥ן הָ/אֲרַמִּ֖י ל֥/וֹ לְ/אִשָּֽׁה
verses.en: And Yitschak was forty years old when he took Rivkah, daughter of Betuel the Aramean of Paddan-Aram, sister of Lavan the Aramean, to himself as wife.

verses.he: וַ/יֶּעְתַּ֨ר יִצְחָ֤ק לַֽ/יהוָה֙ לְ/נֹ֣כַח אִשְׁתּ֔/וֹ כִּ֥י עֲקָרָ֖ה הִ֑וא וַ/יֵּעָ֤תֶר ל/וֹ֙ יְהוָ֔ה וַ/תַּ֖הַר רִבְקָ֥ה אִשְׁתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Yitschak pleaded with the LORD on behalf of his wife, for she was barren; and the LORD was entreated by him, and Rivkah his wife conceived.

verses.he: וַ/יִּתְרֹֽצֲצ֤וּ הַ/בָּנִים֙ בְּ/קִרְבָּ֔/הּ וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אִם כֵּ֔ן לָ֥/מָּה זֶּ֖ה אָנֹ֑כִי וַ/תֵּ֖לֶךְ לִ/דְרֹ֥שׁ אֶת יְהוָֽה
verses.en: And the children struggled violently within her, and she said: "If it be so, why am I thus?" And she went to seek the LORD.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יְהוָ֜ה לָ֗/הּ שְׁנֵ֤י גיים גוֹיִם֙ בְּ/בִטְנֵ֔/ךְ וּ/שְׁנֵ֣י לְאֻמִּ֔ים מִ/מֵּעַ֖יִ/ךְ יִפָּרֵ֑דוּ וּ/לְאֹם֙ מִ/לְאֹ֣ם יֶֽאֱמָ֔ץ וְ/רַ֖ב יַעֲבֹ֥ד צָעִֽיר
verses.en: And the LORD said unto her: "Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples from your innards shall be parted; and people over people shall prevail, and the greater shall serve the younger."

verses.he: וַ/יִּמְלְא֥וּ יָמֶ֖י/הָ לָ/לֶ֑דֶת וְ/הִנֵּ֥ה תוֹמִ֖ם בְּ/בִטְנָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And her days to give birth were fulfilled, and behold — twins in her womb.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּצֵ֤א הָ/רִאשׁוֹן֙ אַדְמוֹנִ֔י כֻּלּ֖/וֹ כְּ/אַדֶּ֣רֶת שֵׂעָ֑ר וַ/יִּקְרְא֥וּ שְׁמ֖/וֹ עֵשָֽׂו
verses.en: And the first came out ruddy, all of him like a mantle of hair; and they called his name Esav.

verses.he: וְ/אַֽחֲרֵי כֵ֞ן יָצָ֣א אָחִ֗י/ו וְ/יָד֤/וֹ אֹחֶ֨זֶת֙ בַּ/עֲקֵ֣ב עֵשָׂ֔ו וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖/וֹ יַעֲקֹ֑ב וְ/יִצְחָ֛ק בֶּן שִׁשִּׁ֥ים שָׁנָ֖ה בְּ/לֶ֥דֶת אֹתָֽ/ם
verses.en: And after that came out his brother, and his hand was grasping the heel of Esav; and his name was called Yaakov — "Heel-Grasper." And Yitschak was sixty years old when she bore them.

verses.he: וַֽ/יִּגְדְּלוּ֙ הַ/נְּעָרִ֔ים וַ/יְהִ֣י עֵשָׂ֗ו אִ֛ישׁ יֹדֵ֥עַ צַ֖יִד אִ֣ישׁ שָׂדֶ֑ה וְ/יַעֲקֹב֙ אִ֣ישׁ תָּ֔ם יֹשֵׁ֖ב אֹהָלִֽים
verses.en: And the lads grew up; and Esav became a man knowing the hunt, a man of the field; and Yaakov was a man of wholeness, dwelling in tents.

verses.he: וַ/יֶּאֱהַ֥ב יִצְחָ֛ק אֶת עֵשָׂ֖ו כִּי צַ֣יִד בְּ/פִ֑י/ו וְ/רִבְקָ֖ה אֹהֶ֥בֶת אֶֽת יַעֲקֹֽב
verses.en: And Yitschak loved Esav, for the hunt was in his mouth; but Rivkah loved Yaakov.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֥זֶד יַעֲקֹ֖ב נָזִ֑יד וַ/יָּבֹ֥א עֵשָׂ֛ו מִן הַ/שָּׂדֶ֖ה וְ/ה֥וּא עָיֵֽף
verses.en: And Yaakov boiled a boiled-thing; and Esav came from the field, and he was faint.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר עֵשָׂ֜ו אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֗ב הַלְעִיטֵ֤/נִי נָא֙ מִן הָ/אָדֹ֤ם הָ/אָדֹם֙ הַ/זֶּ֔ה כִּ֥י עָיֵ֖ף אָנֹ֑כִי עַל כֵּ֥ן קָרָֽא שְׁמ֖/וֹ אֱדֽוֹם
verses.en: And Esav said unto Yaakov: "Let me gulp down from this red, this red thing, for I am faint." Therefore his name was called Edom — "Red."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֑ב מִכְרָ֥/ה כַ/יּ֛וֹם אֶת בְּכֹֽרָתְ/ךָ֖ לִֽ/י
verses.en: And Yaakov said: "Sell me, as of this day, your firstborn-right."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר עֵשָׂ֔ו הִנֵּ֛ה אָנֹכִ֥י הוֹלֵ֖ךְ לָ/מ֑וּת וְ/לָ/מָּה זֶּ֥ה לִ֖/י בְּכֹרָֽה
verses.en: And Esav said: "Behold, I am going to die; and of what use is a firstborn-right to me?"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֗ב הִשָּׁ֤בְעָ/ה לִּ/י֙ כַּ/יּ֔וֹם וַ/יִּשָּׁבַ֖ע ל֑/וֹ וַ/יִּמְכֹּ֥ר אֶת בְּכֹרָת֖/וֹ לְ/יַעֲקֹֽב
verses.en: And Yaakov said: "Swear to me as of this day." And he swore to him, and sold his firstborn-right unto Yaakov.

verses.he: וְ/יַעֲקֹ֞ב נָתַ֣ן לְ/עֵשָׂ֗ו לֶ֚חֶם וּ/נְזִ֣יד עֲדָשִׁ֔ים וַ/יֹּ֣אכַל וַ/יֵּ֔שְׁתְּ וַ/יָּ֖קָם וַ/יֵּלַ֑ךְ וַ/יִּ֥בֶז עֵשָׂ֖ו אֶת הַ/בְּכֹרָֽה
verses.en: And Yaakov gave Esav bread and a stew of lentils; and he ate, and he drank, and he rose, and he went. And Esav despised the firstborn-right.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֤י רָעָב֙ בָּ/אָ֔רֶץ מִ/לְּ/בַד֙ הָ/רָעָ֣ב הָ/רִאשׁ֔וֹן אֲשֶׁ֥ר הָיָ֖ה בִּ/ימֵ֣י אַבְרָהָ֑ם וַ/יֵּ֧לֶךְ יִצְחָ֛ק אֶל אֲבִימֶּ֥לֶךְ מֶֽלֶךְ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֖ים גְּרָֽרָ/ה
verses.en: And there was a famine in the land, besides the first famine which was in the days of Avraham. And Yitschak went unto Avimelekh, king of the Pelishtim, to Gerar.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּרָ֤א אֵלָי/ו֙ יְהוָ֔ה וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אַל תֵּרֵ֣ד מִצְרָ֑יְמָ/ה שְׁכֹ֣ן בָּ/אָ֔רֶץ אֲשֶׁ֖ר אֹמַ֥ר אֵלֶֽי/ךָ
verses.en: And the LORD appeared unto him and said: "Do not go down to Mitsrayim; dwell in the land which I shall say unto you.

verses.he: גּ֚וּר בָּ/אָ֣רֶץ הַ/זֹּ֔את וְ/אֶֽהְיֶ֥ה עִמְּ/ךָ֖ וַ/אֲבָרְכֶ֑/ךָּ כִּֽי לְ/ךָ֣ וּֽ/לְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֗ אֶתֵּן֙ אֶת כָּל הָֽ/אֲרָצֹ֣ת הָ/אֵ֔ל וַ/הֲקִֽמֹתִי֙ אֶת הַ/שְּׁבֻעָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר נִשְׁבַּ֖עְתִּי לְ/אַבְרָהָ֥ם אָבִֽי/ךָ
verses.en: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and bless you; for to you and to your seed I will give all these lands, and I will uphold the oath which I swore unto Avraham your father.

verses.he: וְ/הִרְבֵּיתִ֤י אֶֽת זַרְעֲ/ךָ֙ כְּ/כוֹכְבֵ֣י הַ/שָּׁמַ֔יִם וְ/נָתַתִּ֣י לְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ אֵ֥ת כָּל הָ/אֲרָצֹ֖ת הָ/אֵ֑ל וְ/הִתְבָּרֲכ֣וּ בְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ כֹּ֖ל גּוֹיֵ֥י הָ/אָֽרֶץ
verses.en: And I will multiply your seed as the stars of the heavens, and I will give to your seed all these lands; and in your seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed —

verses.he: עֵ֕קֶב אֲשֶׁר שָׁמַ֥ע אַבְרָהָ֖ם בְּ/קֹלִ֑/י וַ/יִּשְׁמֹר֙ מִשְׁמַרְתִּ֔/י מִצְוֺתַ֖/י חֻקּוֹתַ֥/י וְ/תוֹרֹתָֽ/י
verses.en: on account of Avraham hearkening to My voice, and guarding My charge, My commandments, My statutes, and My teachings."

verses.he: וַ/יֵּ֥שֶׁב יִצְחָ֖ק בִּ/גְרָֽר
verses.en: And Yitschak dwelt in Gerar.

verses.he: וַֽ/יִּשְׁאֲל֞וּ אַנְשֵׁ֤י הַ/מָּקוֹם֙ לְ/אִשְׁתּ֔/וֹ וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אֲחֹ֣תִ/י הִ֑וא כִּ֤י יָרֵא֙ לֵ/אמֹ֣ר אִשְׁתִּ֔/י פֶּן יַֽהַרְגֻ֜/נִי אַנְשֵׁ֤י הַ/מָּקוֹם֙ עַל רִבְקָ֔ה כִּֽי טוֹבַ֥ת מַרְאֶ֖ה הִֽיא
verses.en: And the men of the place asked about his wife, and he said: "She is my sister." For he feared to say "my wife," lest the men of the place kill me on account of Rivkah, for she was good of appearance.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י כִּ֣י אָֽרְכוּ ל֥/וֹ שָׁם֙ הַ/יָּמִ֔ים וַ/יַּשְׁקֵ֗ף אֲבִימֶ֨לֶךְ֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים בְּעַ֖ד הַֽ/חַלּ֑וֹן וַ/יַּ֗רְא וְ/הִנֵּ֤ה יִצְחָק֙ מְצַחֵ֔ק אֵ֖ת רִבְקָ֥ה אִשְׁתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And it was, when the days had lengthened for him there, that Avimelekh king of the Pelishtim looked out through the window, and he saw — and behold, Yitschak was laughing-with Rivkah his wife.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֨א אֲבִימֶ֜לֶךְ לְ/יִצְחָ֗ק וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ אַ֣ךְ הִנֵּ֤ה אִשְׁתְּ/ךָ֙ הִ֔וא וְ/אֵ֥יךְ אָמַ֖רְתָּ אֲחֹ֣תִ/י הִ֑וא וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָי/ו֙ יִצְחָ֔ק כִּ֣י אָמַ֔רְתִּי פֶּן אָמ֖וּת עָלֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And Avimelekh called Yitschak and said: "Surely, behold, she is your wife! And how did you say, 'She is my sister'?" And Yitschak said unto him: "Because I said, 'Lest I die on account of her.'"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אֲבִימֶ֔לֶךְ מַה זֹּ֖את עָשִׂ֣יתָ לָּ֑/נוּ כִּ֠/מְעַט שָׁכַ֞ב אַחַ֤ד הָ/עָם֙ אֶת אִשְׁתֶּ֔/ךָ וְ/הֵבֵאתָ֥ עָלֵ֖י/נוּ אָשָֽׁם
verses.en: And Avimelekh said: "What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt-debt upon us."

verses.he: וַ/יְצַ֣ו אֲבִימֶ֔לֶךְ אֶת כָּל הָ/עָ֖ם לֵ/אמֹ֑ר הַ/נֹּגֵ֜עַ בָּ/אִ֥ישׁ הַ/זֶּ֛ה וּ/בְ/אִשְׁתּ֖/וֹ מ֥וֹת יוּמָֽת
verses.en: And Avimelekh commanded all the people, saying: "Whoever touches this man or his wife shall surely be put to death."

verses.he: וַ/יִּזְרַ֤ע יִצְחָק֙ בָּ/אָ֣רֶץ הַ/הִ֔וא וַ/יִּמְצָ֛א בַּ/שָּׁנָ֥ה הַ/הִ֖וא מֵאָ֣ה שְׁעָרִ֑ים וַֽ/יְבָרֲכֵ֖/הוּ יְהוָֽה
verses.en: And Yitschak sowed in that land, and found in that year a hundredfold; and the LORD blessed him.

verses.he: וַ/יִּגְדַּ֖ל הָ/אִ֑ישׁ וַ/יֵּ֤לֶךְ הָלוֹךְ֙ וְ/גָדֵ֔ל עַ֥ד כִּֽי גָדַ֖ל מְאֹֽד
verses.en: And the man grew great, and went on growing greater, until he was very great.

verses.he: וַֽ/יְהִי ל֤/וֹ מִקְנֵה צֹאן֙ וּ/מִקְנֵ֣ה בָקָ֔ר וַ/עֲבֻדָּ֖ה רַבָּ֑ה וַ/יְקַנְא֥וּ אֹת֖/וֹ פְּלִשְׁתִּֽים
verses.en: And he had possessions of flocks, and possessions of herds, and a great household; and the Pelishtim envied him.

verses.he: וְ/כָל הַ/בְּאֵרֹ֗ת אֲשֶׁ֤ר חָֽפְרוּ֙ עַבְדֵ֣י אָבִ֔י/ו בִּ/ימֵ֖י אַבְרָהָ֣ם אָבִ֑י/ו סִתְּמ֣וּ/ם פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וַ/יְמַלְא֖וּ/ם עָפָֽר
verses.en: And all the wells which the servants of his father had dug in the days of Avraham his father — the Pelishtim had stopped them up and filled them with earth.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֲבִימֶ֖לֶךְ אֶל יִצְחָ֑ק לֵ֚ךְ מֵֽ/עִמָּ֔/נוּ כִּֽי עָצַֽמְתָּ מִמֶּ֖/נּוּ מְאֹֽד
verses.en: And Avimelekh said unto Yitschak: "Go away from us, for you have become far mightier than we."
And Yitschak went from there, and he encamped in the valley of Gerar, and dwelt there.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֨שָׁב יִצְחָ֜ק וַ/יַּחְפֹּ֣ר אֶת בְּאֵרֹ֣ת הַ/מַּ֗יִם אֲשֶׁ֤ר חָֽפְרוּ֙ בִּ/ימֵי֙ אַבְרָהָ֣ם אָבִ֔י/ו וַ/יְסַתְּמ֣וּ/ם פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים אַחֲרֵ֖י מ֣וֹת אַבְרָהָ֑ם וַ/יִּקְרָ֤א לָ/הֶן֙ שֵׁמ֔וֹת כַּ/שֵּׁמֹ֕ת אֲשֶׁר קָרָ֥א לָ/הֶ֖ן אָבִֽי/ו
verses.en: And Yitschak returned and dug again the wells of water which they had dug in the days of Avraham his father, and which the Pelishtim had stopped up after the death of Avraham; and he called them by the names, the same names by which his father had called them.

verses.he: וַ/יַּחְפְּר֥וּ עַבְדֵֽי יִצְחָ֖ק בַּ/נָּ֑חַל וַ/יִּ֨מְצְאוּ שָׁ֔ם בְּאֵ֖ר מַ֥יִם חַיִּֽים
verses.en: And the servants of Yitschak dug in the valley, and they found there a well of living water.

verses.he: וַ/יָּרִ֜יבוּ רֹעֵ֣י גְרָ֗ר עִם רֹעֵ֥י יִצְחָ֛ק לֵ/אמֹ֖ר לָ֣/נוּ הַ/מָּ֑יִם וַ/יִּקְרָ֤א שֵֽׁם הַ/בְּאֵר֙ עֵ֔שֶׂק כִּ֥י הִֽתְעַשְּׂק֖וּ עִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And the shepherds of Gerar quarreled with the shepherds of Yitschak, saying: "The water is ours." And he called the name of the well Esek — "Contention" — for they contended with him.

verses.he: וַֽ/יַּחְפְּרוּ֙ בְּאֵ֣ר אַחֶ֔רֶת וַ/יָּרִ֖יבוּ גַּם עָלֶ֑י/הָ וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמָ֖/הּ שִׂטְנָֽה
verses.en: And they dug another well, and they quarreled over it also; and he called its name Sitnah — "Accusation."

verses.he: וַ/יַּעְתֵּ֣ק מִ/שָּׁ֗ם וַ/יַּחְפֹּר֙ בְּאֵ֣ר אַחֶ֔רֶת וְ/לֹ֥א רָב֖וּ עָלֶ֑י/הָ וַ/יִּקְרָ֤א שְׁמָ/הּ֙ רְחֹב֔וֹת וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר כִּֽי עַתָּ֞ה הִרְחִ֧יב יְהוָ֛ה לָ֖/נוּ וּ/פָרִ֥ינוּ בָ/אָֽרֶץ
verses.en: And he moved from there, and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it; and he called its name Rechovot — "Wide-Spaces" — and said: "For now the LORD has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land."
And he went up from there to Beer-Sheva.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּרָ֨א אֵלָ֤י/ו יְהוָה֙ בַּ/לַּ֣יְלָה הַ/ה֔וּא וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אָנֹכִ֕י אֱלֹהֵ֖י אַבְרָהָ֣ם אָבִ֑י/ךָ אַל תִּירָא֙ כִּֽי אִתְּ/ךָ֣ אָנֹ֔כִי וּ/בֵֽרַכְתִּ֨י/ךָ֙ וְ/הִרְבֵּיתִ֣י אֶֽת זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ בַּ/עֲב֖וּר אַבְרָהָ֥ם עַבְדִּֽ/י
verses.en: And the LORD appeared unto him that night and said: "I am the God of Avraham your father. Do not fear, for I am with you; and I will bless you and multiply your seed, for the sake of Avraham My servant."

verses.he: וַ/יִּ֧בֶן שָׁ֣ם מִזְבֵּ֗חַ וַ/יִּקְרָא֙ בְּ/שֵׁ֣ם יְהוָ֔ה וַ/יֶּט שָׁ֖ם אָהֳל֑/וֹ וַ/יִּכְרוּ שָׁ֥ם עַבְדֵי יִצְחָ֖ק בְּאֵֽר
verses.en: And he built there a slaughter-place, and called upon the name of the LORD, and pitched his tent there; and the servants of Yitschak dug there a well.

verses.he: וַ/אֲבִימֶ֕לֶךְ הָלַ֥ךְ אֵלָ֖י/ו מִ/גְּרָ֑ר וַ/אֲחֻזַּת֙ מֵֽרֵעֵ֔/הוּ וּ/פִיכֹ֖ל שַׂר צְבָאֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Avimelekh went unto him from Gerar, and Achuzzat his companion, and Pikhol the commander of his army.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֲלֵ/הֶם֙ יִצְחָ֔ק מַדּ֖וּעַ בָּאתֶ֣ם אֵלָ֑/י וְ/אַתֶּם֙ שְׂנֵאתֶ֣ם אֹתִ֔/י וַ/תְּשַׁלְּח֖וּ/נִי מֵ/אִתְּ/כֶֽם
verses.en: And Yitschak said unto them: "Why have you come unto me, seeing that you hated me and sent me away from you?"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמְר֗וּ רָא֣וֹ רָאִינוּ֮ כִּֽי הָיָ֣ה יְהוָ֣ה עִמָּ/ךְ֒ וַ/נֹּ֗אמֶר תְּהִ֨י נָ֥א אָלָ֛ה בֵּינוֹתֵ֖י/נוּ בֵּינֵ֣י/נוּ וּ/בֵינֶ֑/ךָ וְ/נִכְרְתָ֥ה בְרִ֖ית עִמָּֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And they said: "We have seen, yes seen, that the LORD has been with you; and we said, 'Let there be, now, a sworn-oath between us, between us and you, and let us cut a cutting-bond with you —

verses.he: אִם תַּעֲשֵׂ֨ה עִמָּ֜/נוּ רָעָ֗ה כַּ/אֲשֶׁר֙ לֹ֣א נְגַֽעֲנ֔וּ/ךָ וְ/כַ/אֲשֶׁ֨ר עָשִׂ֤ינוּ עִמְּ/ךָ֙ רַק ט֔וֹב וַ/נְּשַׁלֵּֽחֲ/ךָ֖ בְּ/שָׁל֑וֹם אַתָּ֥ה עַתָּ֖ה בְּר֥וּךְ יְהוָֽה
verses.en: that you will do us no evil, just as we have not touched you, and just as we have done to you only good and have sent you away in wholeness. You are now the blessed of the LORD.'"

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֤עַשׂ לָ/הֶם֙ מִשְׁתֶּ֔ה וַ/יֹּאכְל֖וּ וַ/יִּשְׁתּֽוּ
verses.en: And he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.

verses.he: וַ/יַּשְׁכִּ֣ימוּ בַ/בֹּ֔קֶר וַ/יִּשָּׁבְע֖וּ אִ֣ישׁ לְ/אָחִ֑י/ו וַ/יְשַׁלְּחֵ֣/ם יִצְחָ֔ק וַ/יֵּלְכ֥וּ מֵ/אִתּ֖/וֹ בְּ/שָׁלֽוֹם
verses.en: And they arose early in the morning, and swore each to his brother; and Yitschak sent them away, and they went from him in wholeness.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֣י בַּ/יּ֣וֹם הַ/ה֗וּא וַ/יָּבֹ֨אוּ֙ עַבְדֵ֣י יִצְחָ֔ק וַ/יַּגִּ֣דוּ ל֔/וֹ עַל אֹד֥וֹת הַ/בְּאֵ֖ר אֲשֶׁ֣ר חָפָ֑רוּ וַ/יֹּ֥אמְרוּ ל֖/וֹ מָצָ֥אנוּ מָֽיִם
verses.en: And it was on that day, that the servants of Yitschak came and told him concerning the well which they had dug, and they said to him: "We have found water."

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א אֹתָ֖/הּ שִׁבְעָ֑ה עַל כֵּ֤ן שֵׁם הָ/עִיר֙ בְּאֵ֣ר שֶׁ֔בַע עַ֖ד הַ/יּ֥וֹם הַ/זֶּֽה
verses.en: And he called it Shivah — "Seven" — therefore the name of the city is Beer-Sheva — "Well of the Oath" — unto this day.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֤י עֵשָׂו֙ בֶּן אַרְבָּעִ֣ים שָׁנָ֔ה וַ/יִּקַּ֤ח אִשָּׁה֙ אֶת יְהוּדִ֔ית בַּת בְּאֵרִ֖י הַֽ/חִתִּ֑י וְ/אֶת בָּ֣שְׂמַ֔ת בַּת אֵילֹ֖ן הַֽ/חִתִּֽי
verses.en: And Esav was forty years old, and he took as wife Yehudit daughter of Beeri the Chittite, and Basemat daughter of Elon the Chittite.

verses.he: וַ/תִּהְיֶ֖יןָ מֹ֣רַת ר֑וּחַ לְ/יִצְחָ֖ק וּ/לְ/רִבְקָֽה
verses.en: And they were a bitterness of spirit unto Yitschak and unto Rivkah.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִי֙ כִּֽי זָקֵ֣ן יִצְחָ֔ק וַ/תִּכְהֶ֥יןָ עֵינָ֖י/ו מֵ/רְאֹ֑ת וַ/יִּקְרָ֞א אֶת עֵשָׂ֣ו בְּנ֣/וֹ הַ/גָּדֹ֗ל וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָי/ו֙ בְּנִ֔/י וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֵלָ֖י/ו הִנֵּֽנִ/י
verses.en: And it was, when Yitschak was old and his eyes were too dim to see, that he called Esav his elder son and said unto him: "My son." And he said unto him: "Here I am."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר הִנֵּה נָ֖א זָקַ֑נְתִּי לֹ֥א יָדַ֖עְתִּי י֥וֹם מוֹתִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said: "Behold now, I am old; I do not know the day of my death.

verses.he: וְ/עַתָּה֙ שָׂא נָ֣א כֵלֶ֔י/ךָ תֶּלְיְ/ךָ֖ וְ/קַשְׁתֶּ֑/ךָ וְ/צֵא֙ הַ/שָּׂדֶ֔ה וְ/צ֥וּדָ/ה לִּ֖/י צידה צָֽיִד
verses.en: And now, take up, I pray, your gear — your quiver and your bow — and go out to the field, and hunt me a hunt.

verses.he: וַ/עֲשֵׂה לִ֨/י מַטְעַמִּ֜ים כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָהַ֛בְתִּי וְ/הָבִ֥יאָ/ה לִּ֖/י וְ/אֹכֵ֑לָה בַּ/עֲב֛וּר תְּבָרֶכְ/ךָ֥ נַפְשִׁ֖/י בְּ/טֶ֥רֶם אָמֽוּת
verses.en: And make me delicacies, such as I love, and bring them to me, and I will eat, so that my breath may bless you before I die."

verses.he: וְ/רִבְקָ֣ה שֹׁמַ֔עַת בְּ/דַבֵּ֣ר יִצְחָ֔ק אֶל עֵשָׂ֖ו בְּנ֑/וֹ וַ/יֵּ֤לֶךְ עֵשָׂו֙ הַ/שָּׂדֶ֔ה לָ/צ֥וּד צַ֖יִד לְ/הָבִֽיא
verses.en: And Rivkah was hearing when Yitschak spoke unto Esav his son. And Esav went to the field to hunt a hunt, to bring it.

verses.he: וְ/רִבְקָה֙ אָֽמְרָ֔ה אֶל יַעֲקֹ֥ב בְּנָ֖/הּ לֵ/אמֹ֑ר הִנֵּ֤ה שָׁמַ֨עְתִּי֙ אֶת אָבִ֔י/ךָ מְדַבֵּ֛ר אֶל עֵשָׂ֥ו אָחִ֖י/ךָ לֵ/אמֹֽר
verses.en: And Rivkah said unto Yaakov her son, saying: "Behold, I heard your father speaking unto Esav your brother, saying:

verses.he: הָבִ֨יאָ/ה לִּ֥/י צַ֛יִד וַ/עֲשֵׂה לִ֥/י מַטְעַמִּ֖ים וְ/אֹכֵ֑לָה וַ/אֲבָרֶכְ/כָ֛ה לִ/פְנֵ֥י יְהוָ֖ה לִ/פְנֵ֥י מוֹתִֽ/י
verses.en: 'Bring me game and make me delicacies, and I will eat, and I will bless you before the LORD, before my death.'

verses.he: וְ/עַתָּ֥ה בְנִ֖/י שְׁמַ֣ע בְּ/קֹלִ֑/י לַ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר אֲנִ֖י מְצַוָּ֥ה אֹתָֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And now, my son, heed my voice, in that which I command you.

verses.he: לֶךְ נָא֙ אֶל הַ/צֹּ֔אן וְ/קַֽח לִ֣/י מִ/שָּׁ֗ם שְׁנֵ֛י גְּדָיֵ֥י עִזִּ֖ים טֹבִ֑ים וְ/אֶֽעֱשֶׂ֨ה אֹתָ֧/ם מַטְעַמִּ֛ים לְ/אָבִ֖י/ךָ כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָהֵֽב
verses.en: Go, I pray, to the flock, and take for me from there two good kids of goats, and I will make them into delicacies for your father, such as he loves.

verses.he: וְ/הֵבֵאתָ֥ לְ/אָבִ֖י/ךָ וְ/אָכָ֑ל בַּ/עֲבֻ֛ר אֲשֶׁ֥ר יְבָרֶכְ/ךָ֖ לִ/פְנֵ֥י מוֹתֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And you shall bring them to your father, and he will eat, so that he may bless you before his death."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֔ב אֶל רִבְקָ֖ה אִמּ֑/וֹ הֵ֣ן עֵשָׂ֤ו אָחִ/י֙ אִ֣ישׁ שָׂעִ֔ר וְ/אָנֹכִ֖י אִ֥ישׁ חָלָֽק
verses.en: And Yaakov said unto Rivkah his mother: "Behold, Esav my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.

verses.he: אוּלַ֤י יְמֻשֵּׁ֨/נִי֙ אָבִ֔/י וְ/הָיִ֥יתִי בְ/עֵינָ֖י/ו כִּ/מְתַעְתֵּ֑עַ וְ/הֵבֵאתִ֥י עָלַ֛/י קְלָלָ֖ה וְ/לֹ֥א בְרָכָֽה
verses.en: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall be in his eyes as a mocker, and I shall bring upon myself a curse and not a blessing."

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֤אמֶר ל/וֹ֙ אִמּ֔/וֹ עָלַ֥/י קִלְלָתְ/ךָ֖ בְּנִ֑/י אַ֛ךְ שְׁמַ֥ע בְּ/קֹלִ֖/י וְ/לֵ֥ךְ קַֽח לִֽ/י
verses.en: And his mother said unto him: "Upon me be your curse, my son. Only heed my voice, and go, take for me."

verses.he: וַ/יֵּ֨לֶךְ֙ וַ/יִּקַּ֔ח וַ/יָּבֵ֖א לְ/אִמּ֑/וֹ וַ/תַּ֤עַשׂ אִמּ/וֹ֙ מַטְעַמִּ֔ים כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֖ר אָהֵ֥ב אָבִֽי/ו
verses.en: And he went, and took, and brought them to his mother; and his mother made delicacies, such as his father loved.

verses.he: וַ/תִּקַּ֣ח רִ֠בְקָה אֶת בִּגְדֵ֨י עֵשָׂ֜ו בְּנָ֤/הּ הַ/גָּדֹל֙ הַ/חֲמֻדֹ֔ת אֲשֶׁ֥ר אִתָּ֖/הּ בַּ/בָּ֑יִת וַ/תַּלְבֵּ֥שׁ אֶֽת יַעֲקֹ֖ב בְּנָ֥/הּ הַ/קָּטָֽן
verses.en: And Rivkah took the garments of Esav her elder son — the coveted ones — which were with her in the house, and clothed Yaakov her younger son.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗ת עֹרֹת֙ גְּדָיֵ֣י הָֽ/עִזִּ֔ים הִלְבִּ֖ישָׁה עַל יָדָ֑י/ו וְ/עַ֖ל חֶלְקַ֥ת צַוָּארָֽי/ו
verses.en: And the skins of the kids of goats she put upon his hands and upon the smooth of his neck.

verses.he: וַ/תִּתֵּ֧ן אֶת הַ/מַּטְעַמִּ֛ים וְ/אֶת הַ/לֶּ֖חֶם אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֑תָה בְּ/יַ֖ד יַעֲקֹ֥ב בְּנָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And she gave the delicacies and the bread which she had made into the hand of Yaakov her son.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֥א אֶל אָבִ֖י/ו וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אָבִ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר הִנֶּ֔נִּ/י מִ֥י אַתָּ֖ה בְּנִֽ/י
verses.en: And he came unto his father and said: "My father." And he said: "Here I am. Who are you, my son?"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֜ב אֶל אָבִ֗י/ו אָנֹכִי֙ עֵשָׂ֣ו בְּכֹרֶ֔/ךָ עָשִׂ֕יתִי כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבַּ֖רְתָּ אֵלָ֑/י קֽוּם נָ֣א שְׁבָ֗/ה וְ/אָכְלָ/ה֙ מִ/צֵּידִ֔/י בַּ/עֲב֖וּר תְּבָרֲכַ֥/נִּי נַפְשֶֽׁ/ךָ
verses.en: And Yaakov said unto his father: "I am Esav your firstborn. I have done as you spoke unto me. Rise up, I pray, sit and eat from my hunt, so that your breath may bless me."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר יִצְחָק֙ אֶל בְּנ֔/וֹ מַה זֶּ֛ה מִהַ֥רְתָּ לִ/מְצֹ֖א בְּנִ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר כִּ֥י הִקְרָ֛ה יְהוָ֥ה אֱלֹהֶ֖י/ךָ לְ/פָנָֽ/י
verses.en: And Yitschak said unto his son: "How is it that you found it so quickly, my son?" And he said: "Because the LORD your God caused it to happen before me."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר יִצְחָק֙ אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֔ב גְּשָׁ/ה נָּ֥א וַ/אֲמֻֽשְׁ/ךָ֖ בְּנִ֑/י הַֽ/אַתָּ֥ה זֶ֛ה בְּנִ֥/י עֵשָׂ֖ו אִם לֹֽא
verses.en: And Yitschak said unto Yaakov: "Come near, I pray, and let me feel you, my son — are you truly my son Esav, or not?"

verses.he: וַ/יִּגַּ֧שׁ יַעֲקֹ֛ב אֶל יִצְחָ֥ק אָבִ֖י/ו וַ/יְמֻשֵּׁ֑/הוּ וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר הַ/קֹּל֙ ק֣וֹל יַעֲקֹ֔ב וְ/הַ/יָּדַ֖יִם יְדֵ֥י עֵשָֽׂו
verses.en: And Yaakov drew near unto Yitschak his father, and he felt him; and he said: "The voice is the voice of Yaakov, but the hands are the hands of Esav."

verses.he: וְ/לֹ֣א הִכִּיר֔/וֹ כִּֽי הָי֣וּ יָדָ֗י/ו כִּ/ידֵ֛י עֵשָׂ֥ו אָחִ֖י/ו שְׂעִרֹ֑ת וַֽ/יְבָרְכֵֽ/הוּ
verses.en: And he did not recognize him, for his hands were hairy like the hands of Esav his brother; and he blessed him.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אַתָּ֥ה זֶ֖ה בְּנִ֣/י עֵשָׂ֑ו וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אָֽנִי
verses.en: And he said: "Are you truly my son Esav?" And he said: "I am."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר הַגִּ֤שָׁ/ה לִּ/י֙ וְ/אֹֽכְלָה֙ מִ/צֵּ֣יד בְּנִ֔/י לְמַ֥עַן תְּבָֽרֶכְ/ךָ֖ נַפְשִׁ֑/י וַ/יַּגֶּשׁ ל/וֹ֙ וַ/יֹּאכַ֔ל וַ/יָּ֧בֵא ל֦/וֹ יַ֖יִן וַ/יֵּֽשְׁתְּ
verses.en: And he said: "Bring it near to me, and I will eat from the hunt of my son, so that my breath may bless you." And he brought it near to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֵלָ֖י/ו יִצְחָ֣ק אָבִ֑י/ו גְּשָׁ/ה נָּ֥א וּ/שְׁקָ/ה לִּ֖/י בְּנִֽ/י
verses.en: And Yitschak his father said unto him: "Come near, I pray, and kiss me, my son."

verses.he: וַ/יִּגַּשׁ֙ וַ/יִּשַּׁק ל֔/וֹ וַ/יָּ֛רַח אֶת רֵ֥יחַ בְּגָדָ֖י/ו וַֽ/יְבָרֲכֵ֑/הוּ וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר רְאֵה֙ רֵ֣יחַ בְּנִ֔/י כְּ/רֵ֣יחַ שָׂדֶ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר בֵּרֲכ֖/וֹ יְהוָֽה
verses.en: And he came near and kissed him; and he smelled the scent of his garments, and he blessed him, and said: "See, the scent of my son is as the scent of a field which the LORD has blessed.

verses.he: וְ/יִֽתֶּן לְ/ךָ֙ הָ/אֱלֹהִ֔ים מִ/טַּל֙ הַ/שָּׁמַ֔יִם וּ/מִ/שְׁמַנֵּ֖י הָ/אָ֑רֶץ וְ/רֹ֥ב דָּגָ֖ן וְ/תִירֹֽשׁ
verses.en: And may God give you of the dew of the heavens, and of the fatness of the earth, and abundance of grain and new wine.

verses.he: יַֽעַבְד֣וּ/ךָ עַמִּ֗ים ו/ישתחו וְ/יִֽשְׁתַּחֲו֤וּ לְ/ךָ֙ לְאֻמִּ֔ים הֱוֵ֤ה גְבִיר֙ לְ/אַחֶ֔י/ךָ וְ/יִשְׁתַּחֲוּ֥וּ לְ/ךָ֖ בְּנֵ֣י אִמֶּ֑/ךָ אֹרְרֶ֣י/ךָ אָר֔וּר וּֽ/מְבָרֲכֶ֖י/ךָ בָּרֽוּךְ
verses.en: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers, and let the sons of your mother bow down to you. Those who curse you — cursed; and those who bless you — blessed."

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֨ר כִּלָּ֣ה יִצְחָק֮ לְ/בָרֵ֣ךְ אֶֽת יַעֲקֹב֒ וַ/יְהִ֗י אַ֣ךְ יָצֹ֤א יָצָא֙ יַעֲקֹ֔ב מֵ/אֵ֥ת פְּנֵ֖י יִצְחָ֣ק אָבִ֑י/ו וְ/עֵשָׂ֣ו אָחִ֔י/ו בָּ֖א מִ/צֵּידֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And it was, as Yitschak had finished, yes finished, blessing Yaakov — and Yaakov had just, yes just, gone out from the face of Yitschak his father — that Esav his brother came in from his hunt.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֤עַשׂ גַּם הוּא֙ מַטְעַמִּ֔ים וַ/יָּבֵ֖א לְ/אָבִ֑י/ו וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לְ/אָבִ֗י/ו יָקֻ֤ם אָבִ/י֙ וְ/יֹאכַל֙ מִ/צֵּ֣יד בְּנ֔/וֹ בַּ/עֲב֖וּר תְּבָרֲכַ֥/נִּי נַפְשֶֽׁ/ךָ
verses.en: And he also made delicacies, and brought them to his father, and said to his father: "Let my father arise and eat from the hunt of his son, so that your breath may bless me."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר ל֛/וֹ יִצְחָ֥ק אָבִ֖י/ו מִי אָ֑תָּה וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אֲנִ֛י בִּנְ/ךָ֥ בְכֹֽרְ/ךָ֖ עֵשָֽׂו
verses.en: And Yitschak his father said to him: "Who are you?" And he said: "I am your son, your firstborn, Esav."

verses.he: וַ/יֶּחֱרַ֨ד יִצְחָ֣ק חֲרָדָה֮ גְּדֹלָ֣ה עַד מְאֹד֒ וַ/יֹּ֡אמֶר מִֽי אֵפ֡וֹא ה֣וּא הַ/צָּֽד צַיִד֩ וַ/יָּ֨בֵא לִ֜/י וָ/אֹכַ֥ל מִ/כֹּ֛ל בְּ/טֶ֥רֶם תָּב֖וֹא וָ/אֲבָרֲכֵ֑/הוּ גַּם בָּר֖וּךְ יִהְיֶֽה
verses.en: And Yitschak trembled a trembling great beyond measure, and said: "Who then is the one who hunted a hunt and brought it to me, and I ate of all before you came, and I blessed him? Indeed, blessed he shall be."

verses.he: כִּ/שְׁמֹ֤עַ עֵשָׂו֙ אֶת דִּבְרֵ֣י אָבִ֔י/ו וַ/יִּצְעַ֣ק צְעָקָ֔ה גְּדֹלָ֥ה וּ/מָרָ֖ה עַד מְאֹ֑ד וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לְ/אָבִ֔י/ו בָּרֲכֵ֥/נִי גַם אָ֖נִי אָבִֽ/י
verses.en: When Esav heard the words of his father, he cried a cry great and exceedingly bitter, and said to his father: "Bless me — me also — my father!"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר בָּ֥א אָחִ֖י/ךָ בְּ/מִרְמָ֑ה וַ/יִּקַּ֖ח בִּרְכָתֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And he said: "Your brother came in deceit, and took your blessing."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֡אמֶר הֲ/כִי֩ קָרָ֨א שְׁמ֜/וֹ יַעֲקֹ֗ב וַֽ/יַּעְקְבֵ֨/נִי֙ זֶ֣ה פַעֲמַ֔יִם אֶת בְּכֹרָתִ֣/י לָקָ֔ח וְ/הִנֵּ֥ה עַתָּ֖ה לָקַ֣ח בִּרְכָתִ֑/י וַ/יֹּאמַ֕ר הֲ/לֹא אָצַ֥לְתָּ לִּ֖/י בְּרָכָֽה
verses.en: And he said: "Is it because his name was called Yaakov that he has heel-tripped me these two times? My firstborn-right he took, and behold, now he has taken my blessing." And he said: "Have you not set aside a blessing for me?"

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֨עַן יִצְחָ֜ק וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לְ/עֵשָׂ֗ו הֵ֣ן גְּבִ֞יר שַׂמְתִּ֥י/ו לָ/ךְ֙ וְ/אֶת כָּל אֶחָ֗י/ו נָתַ֤תִּי ל/וֹ֙ לַ/עֲבָדִ֔ים וְ/דָגָ֥ן וְ/תִירֹ֖שׁ סְמַכְתִּ֑י/ו וּ/לְ/כָ֣ה אֵפ֔וֹא מָ֥ה אֶֽעֱשֶׂ֖ה בְּנִֽ/י
verses.en: And Yitschak answered and said unto Esav: "Behold, I have set him as lord over you, and all his brothers I have given him as servants, and with grain and new wine I have sustained him — and for you, then, what can I do, my son?"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר עֵשָׂ֜ו אֶל אָבִ֗י/ו הַֽ/בְרָכָ֨ה אַחַ֤ת הִֽוא לְ/ךָ֙ אָבִ֔/י בָּרֲכֵ֥/נִי גַם אָ֖נִי אָבִ֑/י וַ/יִּשָּׂ֥א עֵשָׂ֛ו קֹל֖/וֹ וַ/יֵּֽבְךְּ
verses.en: And Esav said unto his father: "Is there but one blessing for you, my father? Bless me — me also — my father!" And Esav lifted his voice and wept.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֛עַן יִצְחָ֥ק אָבִ֖י/ו וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֑י/ו הִנֵּ֞ה מִ/שְׁמַנֵּ֤י הָ/אָ֨רֶץ֙ יִהְיֶ֣ה מֽוֹשָׁבֶ֔/ךָ וּ/מִ/טַּ֥ל הַ/שָּׁמַ֖יִם מֵ/עָֽל
verses.en: And Yitschak his father answered and said unto him: "Behold, away from the fatness of the earth shall be your dwelling, and away from the dew of the heavens above.

verses.he: וְ/עַל חַרְבְּ/ךָ֣ תִֽחְיֶ֔ה וְ/אֶת אָחִ֖י/ךָ תַּעֲבֹ֑ד וְ/הָיָה֙ כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֣ר תָּרִ֔יד וּ/פָרַקְתָּ֥ עֻלּ֖/וֹ מֵ/עַ֥ל צַוָּארֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And by your sword you shall live, and your brother you shall serve; and it shall be, when you break free, that you shall tear his yoke from upon your neck."

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׂטֹ֤ם עֵשָׂו֙ אֶֽת יַעֲקֹ֔ב עַל הַ֨/בְּרָכָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר בֵּרֲכ֖/וֹ אָבִ֑י/ו וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר עֵשָׂ֜ו בְּ/לִבּ֗/וֹ יִקְרְבוּ֙ יְמֵי֙ אֵ֣בֶל אָבִ֔/י וְ/אַֽהַרְגָ֖ה אֶת יַעֲקֹ֥ב אָחִֽ/י
verses.en: And Esav harbored enmity against Yaakov on account of the blessing with which his father had blessed him; and Esav said in his heart: "The days of mourning for my father draw near; then I will kill Yaakov my brother."

verses.he: וַ/יֻּגַּ֣ד לְ/רִבְקָ֔ה אֶת דִּבְרֵ֥י עֵשָׂ֖ו בְּנָ֣/הּ הַ/גָּדֹ֑ל וַ/תִּשְׁלַ֞ח וַ/תִּקְרָ֤א לְ/יַעֲקֹב֙ בְּנָ֣/הּ הַ/קָּטָ֔ן וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֔י/ו הִנֵּה֙ עֵשָׂ֣ו אָחִ֔י/ךָ מִתְנַחֵ֥ם לְ/ךָ֖ לְ/הָרְגֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And the words of Esav her elder son were told to Rivkah; and she sent and called Yaakov her younger son, and said unto him: "Behold, Esav your brother is consoling himself concerning you, to kill you.

verses.he: וְ/עַתָּ֥ה בְנִ֖/י שְׁמַ֣ע בְּ/קֹלִ֑/י וְ/ק֧וּם בְּרַח לְ/ךָ֛ אֶל לָבָ֥ן אָחִ֖/י חָרָֽנָ/ה
verses.en: And now, my son, heed my voice: arise, flee for yourself to Lavan my brother, to Charan.

verses.he: וְ/יָשַׁבְתָּ֥ עִמּ֖/וֹ יָמִ֣ים אֲחָדִ֑ים עַ֥ד אֲשֶׁר תָּשׁ֖וּב חֲמַ֥ת אָחִֽי/ךָ
verses.en: And you shall dwell with him some days, until the burning-wrath of your brother returns.

verses.he: עַד שׁ֨וּב אַף אָחִ֜י/ךָ מִמְּ/ךָ֗ וְ/שָׁכַח֙ אֵ֣ת אֲשֶׁר עָשִׂ֣יתָ לּ֔/וֹ וְ/שָׁלַחְתִּ֖י וּ/לְקַחְתִּ֣י/ךָ מִ/שָּׁ֑ם לָ/מָ֥ה אֶשְׁכַּ֛ל גַּם שְׁנֵי/כֶ֖ם י֥וֹם אֶחָֽד
verses.en: Until the turning of your brother's anger from you, and he forgets what you have done to him; then I will send and take you from there. Why should I be bereaved of you both in one day?"

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֤אמֶר רִבְקָה֙ אֶל יִצְחָ֔ק קַ֣צְתִּי בְ/חַיַּ֔/י מִ/פְּנֵ֖י בְּנ֣וֹת חֵ֑ת אִם לֹקֵ֣חַ יַ֠עֲקֹב אִשָּׁ֨ה מִ/בְּנֽוֹת חֵ֤ת כָּ/אֵ֨לֶּה֙ מִ/בְּנ֣וֹת הָ/אָ֔רֶץ לָ֥/מָּה לִּ֖/י חַיִּֽים
verses.en: And Rivkah said unto Yitschak: "I loathe my life on account of the daughters of Chet. If Yaakov takes a wife from the daughters of Chet, such as these, from the daughters of the land — what good is life to me?"

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א יִצְחָ֛ק אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֖ב וַ/יְבָ֣רֶךְ אֹת֑/וֹ וַ/יְצַוֵּ֨/הוּ֙ וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר ל֔/וֹ לֹֽא תִקַּ֥ח אִשָּׁ֖ה מִ/בְּנ֥וֹת כְּנָֽעַן
verses.en: And Yitschak called Yaakov, and blessed him, and commanded him, and said unto him: "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Kenaan.

verses.he: ק֥וּם לֵךְ֙ פַּדֶּ֣נָֽ/ה אֲרָ֔ם בֵּ֥יתָ/ה בְתוּאֵ֖ל אֲבִ֣י אִמֶּ֑/ךָ וְ/קַח לְ/ךָ֤ מִ/שָּׁם֙ אִשָּׁ֔ה מִ/בְּנ֥וֹת לָבָ֖ן אֲחִ֥י אִמֶּֽ/ךָ
verses.en: Arise, go to Paddan-Aram, to the house of Betuel, father of your mother, and take for yourself from there a wife, from the daughters of Lavan, brother of your mother.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֤ל שַׁדַּי֙ יְבָרֵ֣ךְ אֹֽתְ/ךָ֔ וְ/יַפְרְ/ךָ֖ וְ/יַרְבֶּ֑/ךָ וְ/הָיִ֖יתָ לִ/קְהַ֥ל עַמִּֽים
verses.en: And El Shaddai — God of Sufficiency — shall bless you, and make you fruitful, and multiply you, that you may become an assembly of peoples.

verses.he: וְ/יִֽתֶּן לְ/ךָ֙ אֶת בִּרְכַּ֣ת אַבְרָהָ֔ם לְ/ךָ֖ וּ/לְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֣ אִתָּ֑/ךְ לְ/רִשְׁתְּ/ךָ֙ אֶת אֶ֣רֶץ מְגֻרֶ֔י/ךָ אֲשֶׁר נָתַ֥ן אֱלֹהִ֖ים לְ/אַבְרָהָֽם
verses.en: And may He give you the blessing of Avraham, to you and to your seed with you, that you may possess the land of your sojournings, which God gave to Avraham."

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁלַ֤ח יִצְחָק֙ אֶֽת יַעֲקֹ֔ב וַ/יֵּ֖לֶךְ פַּדֶּ֣נָֽ/ה אֲרָ֑ם אֶל לָבָ֤ן בֶּן בְּתוּאֵל֙ הָֽ/אֲרַמִּ֔י אֲחִ֣י רִבְקָ֔ה אֵ֥ם יַעֲקֹ֖ב וְ/עֵשָֽׂו
verses.en: And Yitschak sent Yaakov away, and he went to Paddan-Aram, unto Lavan son of Betuel the Aramean, brother of Rivkah, mother of Yaakov and Esav.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֣רְא עֵשָׂ֗ו כִּֽי בֵרַ֣ךְ יִצְחָק֮ אֶֽת יַעֲקֹב֒ וְ/שִׁלַּ֤ח אֹת/וֹ֙ פַּדֶּ֣נָֽ/ה אֲרָ֔ם לָ/קַֽחַת ל֥/וֹ מִ/שָּׁ֖ם אִשָּׁ֑ה בְּ/בָרֲכ֣/וֹ אֹת֔/וֹ וַ/יְצַ֤ו עָלָי/ו֙ לֵ/אמֹ֔ר לֹֽא תִקַּ֥ח אִשָּׁ֖ה מִ/בְּנ֥וֹת כְּנָֽעַן
verses.en: And Esav saw that Yitschak had blessed Yaakov and sent him away to Paddan-Aram, to take for himself from there a wife — in blessing him, he commanded him, saying: "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Kenaan" —

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁמַ֣ע יַעֲקֹ֔ב אֶל אָבִ֖י/ו וְ/אֶל אִמּ֑/וֹ וַ/יֵּ֖לֶךְ פַּדֶּ֥נָֽ/ה אֲרָֽם
verses.en: and that Yaakov heeded his father and his mother, and went to Paddan-Aram.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֣רְא עֵשָׂ֔ו כִּ֥י רָע֖וֹת בְּנ֣וֹת כְּנָ֑עַן בְּ/עֵינֵ֖י יִצְחָ֥ק אָבִֽי/ו
verses.en: And Esav saw that the daughters of Kenaan were evil in the eyes of Yitschak his father.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּ֥לֶךְ עֵשָׂ֖ו אֶל יִשְׁמָעֵ֑אל וַ/יִּקַּ֡ח אֶֽת מָחֲלַ֣ת בַּת יִשְׁמָעֵ֨אל בֶּן אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֲח֧וֹת נְבָי֛וֹת עַל נָשָׁ֖י/ו ל֥/וֹ לְ/אִשָּֽׁה
verses.en: And Esav went unto Yishmael, and took Machalat daughter of Yishmael son of Avraham, sister of Nevayot, in addition to his wives, to himself as wife.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּצֵ֥א יַעֲקֹ֖ב מִ/בְּאֵ֣ר שָׁ֑בַע וַ/יֵּ֖לֶךְ חָרָֽנָ/ה
verses.en: And Yaakov went out from Beer-Sheva, and went toward Charan.

verses.he: וַ/יִּפְגַּ֨ע בַּ/מָּק֜וֹם וַ/יָּ֤לֶן שָׁם֙ כִּי בָ֣א הַ/שֶּׁ֔מֶשׁ וַ/יִּקַּח֙ מֵ/אַבְנֵ֣י הַ/מָּק֔וֹם וַ/יָּ֖שֶׂם מְרַֽאֲשֹׁתָ֑י/ו וַ/יִּשְׁכַּ֖ב בַּ/מָּק֥וֹם הַ/הֽוּא
verses.en: And he encountered the Place, and lodged there, for the sun had set; and he took from the stones of the Place, and set them at his head, and lay down in that Place.

verses.he: וַֽ/יַּחֲלֹ֗ם וְ/הִנֵּ֤ה סֻלָּם֙ מֻצָּ֣ב אַ֔רְצָ/ה וְ/רֹאשׁ֖/וֹ מַגִּ֣יעַ הַ/שָּׁמָ֑יְמָ/ה וְ/הִנֵּה֙ מַלְאֲכֵ֣י אֱלֹהִ֔ים עֹלִ֥ים וְ/יֹרְדִ֖ים בּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And he dreamed — and behold, a stairway set upon the earth, and its head reaching to the heavens; and behold, messengers of God ascending and descending upon it.

verses.he: וְ/הִנֵּ֨ה יְהוָ֜ה נִצָּ֣ב עָלָי/ו֮ וַ/יֹּאמַר֒ אֲנִ֣י יְהוָ֗ה אֱלֹהֵי֙ אַבְרָהָ֣ם אָבִ֔י/ךָ וֵ/אלֹהֵ֖י יִצְחָ֑ק הָ/אָ֗רֶץ אֲשֶׁ֤ר אַתָּה֙ שֹׁכֵ֣ב עָלֶ֔י/הָ לְ/ךָ֥ אֶתְּנֶ֖/נָּה וּ/לְ/זַרְעֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And behold, the LORD was standing over him, and He said: "I am the LORD, God of Avraham your father and God of Yitschak. The land upon which you lie — to you I will give it and to your seed.

verses.he: וְ/הָיָ֤ה זַרְעֲ/ךָ֙ כַּ/עֲפַ֣ר הָ/אָ֔רֶץ וּ/פָרַצְתָּ֛ יָ֥מָּ/ה וָ/קֵ֖דְמָ/ה וְ/צָפֹ֣נָ/ה וָ/נֶ֑גְבָּ/ה וְ/נִבְרֲכ֥וּ בְ/ךָ֛ כָּל מִשְׁפְּחֹ֥ת הָ/אֲדָמָ֖ה וּ/בְ/זַרְעֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And your seed shall be as the dust of the earth, and you shall burst forth westward and eastward, and northward and southward; and in you shall all the families of the ground be blessed, and in your seed.

verses.he: וְ/הִנֵּ֨ה אָנֹכִ֜י עִמָּ֗/ךְ וּ/שְׁמַרְתִּ֨י/ךָ֙ בְּ/כֹ֣ל אֲשֶׁר תֵּלֵ֔ךְ וַ/הֲשִׁ֣בֹתִ֔י/ךָ אֶל הָ/אֲדָמָ֖ה הַ/זֹּ֑את כִּ֚י לֹ֣א אֶֽעֱזָבְ/ךָ֔ עַ֚ד אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִם עָשִׂ֔יתִי אֵ֥ת אֲשֶׁר דִּבַּ֖רְתִּי לָֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And behold, I am with you, and I will guard you in all where you go, and I will bring you back unto this ground; for I will not forsake you, until I have done that which I have spoken to you."

verses.he: וַ/יִּיקַ֣ץ יַעֲקֹב֮ מִ/שְּׁנָת/וֹ֒ וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אָכֵן֙ יֵ֣שׁ יְהוָ֔ה בַּ/מָּק֖וֹם הַ/זֶּ֑ה וְ/אָנֹכִ֖י לֹ֥א יָדָֽעְתִּי
verses.en: And Yaakov awoke from his sleep, and said: "Surely the LORD is in this Place, and I — I did not know."

verses.he: וַ/יִּירָא֙ וַ/יֹּאמַ֔ר מַה נּוֹרָ֖א הַ/מָּק֣וֹם הַ/זֶּ֑ה אֵ֣ין זֶ֗ה כִּ֚י אִם בֵּ֣ית אֱלֹהִ֔ים וְ/זֶ֖ה שַׁ֥עַר הַ/שָּׁמָֽיִם
verses.en: And he was in awe, and said: "How awesome is this Place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of the heavens."

verses.he: וַ/יַּשְׁכֵּ֨ם יַעֲקֹ֜ב בַּ/בֹּ֗קֶר וַ/יִּקַּ֤ח אֶת הָ/אֶ֨בֶן֙ אֲשֶׁר שָׂ֣ם מְרַֽאֲשֹׁתָ֔י/ו וַ/יָּ֥שֶׂם אֹתָ֖/הּ מַצֵּבָ֑ה וַ/יִּצֹ֥ק שֶׁ֖מֶן עַל רֹאשָֽׁ/הּ
verses.en: And Yaakov rose early in the morning, and took the stone which he had set at his head, and set it as a standing-pillar, and poured oil upon its head.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֛א אֶת שֵֽׁם הַ/מָּק֥וֹם הַ/ה֖וּא בֵּֽית אֵ֑ל וְ/אוּלָ֛ם ל֥וּז שֵׁם הָ/עִ֖יר לָ/רִאשֹׁנָֽה
verses.en: And he called the name of that Place Beit-El — "House of God." But the name of the city had been Luz at the first.

verses.he: וַ/יִּדַּ֥ר יַעֲקֹ֖ב נֶ֣דֶר לֵ/אמֹ֑ר אִם יִהְיֶ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֜ים עִמָּדִ֗/י וּ/שְׁמָרַ֨/נִי֙ בַּ/דֶּ֤רֶךְ הַ/זֶּה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָנֹכִ֣י הוֹלֵ֔ךְ וְ/נָֽתַן לִ֥/י לֶ֛חֶם לֶ/אֱכֹ֖ל וּ/בֶ֥גֶד לִ/לְבֹּֽשׁ
verses.en: And Yaakov vowed a vow, saying: "If God will be with me, and will guard me in this way that I am going, and will give me bread to eat and garment to wear,

verses.he: וְ/שַׁבְתִּ֥י בְ/שָׁל֖וֹם אֶל בֵּ֣ית אָבִ֑/י וְ/הָיָ֧ה יְהוָ֛ה לִ֖/י לֵ/אלֹהִֽים
verses.en: and I return in wholeness to the house of my father — then the LORD shall be my God.

verses.he: וְ/הָ/אֶ֣בֶן הַ/זֹּ֗את אֲשֶׁר שַׂ֨מְתִּי֙ מַצֵּבָ֔ה יִהְיֶ֖ה בֵּ֣ית אֱלֹהִ֑ים וְ/כֹל֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר תִּתֶּן לִ֔/י עַשֵּׂ֖ר אֲעַשְּׂרֶ֥/נּוּ לָֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And this stone which I have set as a standing-pillar shall be a house of God; and of all that You give me, I will tithe, yes tithe, unto You."

verses.he: וַ/יִּשָּׂ֥א יַעֲקֹ֖ב רַגְלָ֑י/ו וַ/יֵּ֖לֶךְ אַ֥רְצָ/ה בְנֵי קֶֽדֶם
verses.en: And Yaakov lifted his feet, and went to the land of the sons of the East.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֞רְא וְ/הִנֵּ֧ה בְאֵ֣ר בַּ/שָּׂדֶ֗ה וְ/הִנֵּה שָׁ֞ם שְׁלֹשָׁ֤ה עֶדְרֵי צֹאן֙ רֹבְצִ֣ים עָלֶ֔י/הָ כִּ֚י מִן הַ/בְּאֵ֣ר הַ/הִ֔וא יַשְׁק֖וּ הָ/עֲדָרִ֑ים וְ/הָ/אֶ֥בֶן גְּדֹלָ֖ה עַל פִּ֥י הַ/בְּאֵֽר
verses.en: And he looked, and behold — a well in the field, and behold, three flocks of sheep lying beside it; for from that well they would water the flocks; and a great stone was upon the mouth of the well.

verses.he: וְ/נֶאֶסְפוּ שָׁ֣מָּ/ה כָל הָ/עֲדָרִ֗ים וְ/גָלֲל֤וּ אֶת הָ/אֶ֨בֶן֙ מֵ/עַל֙ פִּ֣י הַ/בְּאֵ֔ר וְ/הִשְׁק֖וּ אֶת הַ/צֹּ֑אן וְ/הֵשִׁ֧יבוּ אֶת הָ/אֶ֛בֶן עַל פִּ֥י הַ/בְּאֵ֖ר לִ/מְקֹמָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And all the flocks would be gathered there, and they would roll the stone from upon the mouth of the well, and water the sheep, and return the stone upon the mouth of the well, to its place.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר לָ/הֶם֙ יַעֲקֹ֔ב אַחַ֖/י מֵ/אַ֣יִן אַתֶּ֑ם וַ/יֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ מֵ/חָרָ֖ן אֲנָֽחְנוּ
verses.en: And Yaakov said to them: "My brothers, from where are you?" And they said: "From Charan are we."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לָ/הֶ֔ם הַ/יְדַעְתֶּ֖ם אֶת לָבָ֣ן בֶּן נָח֑וֹר וַ/יֹּאמְר֖וּ יָדָֽעְנוּ
verses.en: And he said to them: "Do you know Lavan son of Nachor?" And they said: "We know him."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר לָ/הֶ֖ם הֲ/שָׁל֣וֹם ל֑/וֹ וַ/יֹּאמְר֣וּ שָׁל֔וֹם וְ/הִנֵּה֙ רָחֵ֣ל בִּתּ֔/וֹ בָּאָ֖ה עִם הַ/צֹּֽאן
verses.en: And he said to them: "Is there wholeness with him?" And they said: "Wholeness — and behold, Rachel his daughter is coming with the flock."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר הֵ֥ן עוֹד֙ הַ/יּ֣וֹם גָּד֔וֹל לֹא עֵ֖ת הֵאָסֵ֣ף הַ/מִּקְנֶ֑ה הַשְׁק֥וּ הַ/צֹּ֖אן וּ/לְכ֥וּ רְעֽוּ
verses.en: And he said: "Behold, the day is yet great; it is not time for the livestock to be gathered. Water the flock, and go, pasture."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמְרוּ֮ לֹ֣א נוּכַל֒ עַ֣ד אֲשֶׁ֤ר יֵאָֽסְפוּ֙ כָּל הָ֣/עֲדָרִ֔ים וְ/גָֽלֲלוּ֙ אֶת הָ/אֶ֔בֶן מֵ/עַ֖ל פִּ֣י הַ/בְּאֵ֑ר וְ/הִשְׁקִ֖ינוּ הַ/צֹּֽאן
verses.en: And they said: "We cannot, until all the flocks are gathered, and they roll the stone from upon the mouth of the well; then we water the flock."

verses.he: עוֹדֶ֖/נּוּ מְדַבֵּ֣ר עִמָּ֑/ם וְ/רָחֵ֣ל בָּ֗אָה עִם הַ/צֹּאן֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְ/אָבִ֔י/הָ כִּ֥י רֹעָ֖ה הִֽוא
verses.en: While he was yet speaking with them, Rachel came with the flock that belonged to her father, for she was a shepherdess.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֡י כַּ/אֲשֶׁר֩ רָאָ֨ה יַעֲקֹ֜ב אֶת רָחֵ֗ל בַּת לָבָן֙ אֲחִ֣י אִמּ֔/וֹ וְ/אֶת צֹ֥אן לָבָ֖ן אֲחִ֣י אִמּ֑/וֹ וַ/יִּגַּ֣שׁ יַעֲקֹ֗ב וַ/יָּ֤גֶל אֶת הָ/אֶ֨בֶן֙ מֵ/עַל֙ פִּ֣י הַ/בְּאֵ֔ר וַ/יַּ֕שְׁקְ אֶת צֹ֥אן לָבָ֖ן אֲחִ֥י אִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And it was, when Yaakov saw Rachel daughter of Lavan, brother of his mother, and the flock of Lavan brother of his mother, that Yaakov drew near and rolled the stone from upon the mouth of the well, and watered the flock of Lavan, brother of his mother.
And Yaakov kissed Rachel, and lifted his voice, and wept.

verses.he: וַ/יַּגֵּ֨ד יַעֲקֹ֜ב לְ/רָחֵ֗ל כִּ֣י אֲחִ֤י אָבִ֨י/הָ֙ ה֔וּא וְ/כִ֥י בֶן רִבְקָ֖ה ה֑וּא וַ/תָּ֖רָץ וַ/תַּגֵּ֥ד לְ/אָבִֽי/הָ
verses.en: And Yaakov told Rachel that he was a kinsman of her father, and that he was the son of Rivkah; and she ran and told her father.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִי֩ כִ/שְׁמֹ֨עַ לָבָ֜ן אֶת שֵׁ֣מַע יַעֲקֹ֣ב בֶּן אֲחֹת֗/וֹ וַ/יָּ֤רָץ לִ/קְרָאת/וֹ֙ וַ/יְחַבֶּק ל/וֹ֙ וַ/יְנַשֶּׁק ל֔/וֹ וַ/יְבִיאֵ֖/הוּ אֶל בֵּית֑/וֹ וַ/יְסַפֵּ֣ר לְ/לָבָ֔ן אֵ֥ת כָּל הַ/דְּבָרִ֖ים הָ/אֵֽלֶּה
verses.en: And it was, when Lavan heard the report of Yaakov, son of his sister, that he ran to meet him, and embraced him, and kissed him, and brought him unto his house. And he recounted to Lavan all these things.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר ל/וֹ֙ לָבָ֔ן אַ֛ךְ עַצְמִ֥/י וּ/בְשָׂרִ֖/י אָ֑תָּה וַ/יֵּ֥שֶׁב עִמּ֖/וֹ חֹ֥דֶשׁ יָמִֽים
verses.en: And Lavan said to him: "Surely you are my bone and my flesh." And he dwelt with him a month of days.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר לָבָן֙ לְ/יַעֲקֹ֔ב הֲ/כִי אָחִ֣/י אַ֔תָּה וַ/עֲבַדְתַּ֖/נִי חִנָּ֑ם הַגִּ֥ידָ/ה לִּ֖/י מַה מַּשְׂכֻּרְתֶּֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And Lavan said unto Yaakov: "Because you are my kinsman, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?"

verses.he: וּ/לְ/לָבָ֖ן שְׁתֵּ֣י בָנ֑וֹת שֵׁ֤ם הַ/גְּדֹלָה֙ לֵאָ֔ה וְ/שֵׁ֥ם הַ/קְּטַנָּ֖ה רָחֵֽל
verses.en: And Lavan had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.

verses.he: וְ/עֵינֵ֥י לֵאָ֖ה רַכּ֑וֹת וְ/רָחֵל֙ הָֽיְתָ֔ה יְפַת תֹּ֖אַר וִ/יפַ֥ת מַרְאֶֽה
verses.en: And the eyes of Leah were tender; but Rachel was beautiful of form and beautiful of appearance.

verses.he: וַ/יֶּאֱהַ֥ב יַעֲקֹ֖ב אֶת רָחֵ֑ל וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר אֶֽעֱבָדְ/ךָ֙ שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֔ים בְּ/רָחֵ֥ל בִּתְּ/ךָ֖ הַ/קְּטַנָּֽה
verses.en: And Yaakov loved Rachel, and said: "I will serve you seven years for Rachel, your younger daughter."

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לָבָ֗ן ט֚וֹב תִּתִּ֣/י אֹתָ֣/הּ לָ֔/ךְ מִ/תִּתִּ֥/י אֹתָ֖/הּ לְ/אִ֣ישׁ אַחֵ֑ר שְׁבָ֖/ה עִמָּדִֽ/י
verses.en: And Lavan said: "Better that I give her to you than that I give her to another man. Dwell with me."

verses.he: וַ/יַּעֲבֹ֧ד יַעֲקֹ֛ב בְּ/רָחֵ֖ל שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֑ים וַ/יִּהְי֤וּ בְ/עֵינָי/ו֙ כְּ/יָמִ֣ים אֲחָדִ֔ים בְּ/אַהֲבָת֖/וֹ אֹתָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And Yaakov served for Rachel seven years, and they were in his eyes as a few days, in his love for her.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֤ב אֶל לָבָן֙ הָבָ֣/ה אֶת אִשְׁתִּ֔/י כִּ֥י מָלְא֖וּ יָמָ֑/י וְ/אָב֖וֹאָה אֵלֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And Yaakov said unto Lavan: "Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, and I will come in unto her."

verses.he: וַ/יֶּאֱסֹ֥ף לָבָ֛ן אֶת כָּל אַנְשֵׁ֥י הַ/מָּק֖וֹם וַ/יַּ֥עַשׂ מִשְׁתֶּֽה
verses.en: And Lavan gathered all the men of the place, and made a feast.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֣י בָ/עֶ֔רֶב וַ/יִּקַּח֙ אֶת לֵאָ֣ה בִתּ֔/וֹ וַ/יָּבֵ֥א אֹתָ֖/הּ אֵלָ֑י/ו וַ/יָּבֹ֖א אֵלֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And it was in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter and brought her unto him; and he came in unto her.

verses.he: וַ/יִּתֵּ֤ן לָבָן֙ לָ֔/הּ אֶת זִלְפָּ֖ה שִׁפְחָת֑/וֹ לְ/לֵאָ֥ה בִתּ֖/וֹ שִׁפְחָֽה
verses.en: And Lavan gave her Zilpah his handmaid, to Leah his daughter, as handmaid.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֣י בַ/בֹּ֔קֶר וְ/הִנֵּה הִ֖וא לֵאָ֑ה וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אֶל לָבָ֗ן מַה זֹּאת֙ עָשִׂ֣יתָ לִּ֔/י הֲ/לֹ֤א בְ/רָחֵל֙ עָבַ֣דְתִּי עִמָּ֔/ךְ וְ/לָ֖/מָּה רִמִּיתָֽ/נִי
verses.en: And it was in the morning — and behold, she was Leah! And he said unto Lavan: "What is this you have done to me? Was it not for Rachel that I served with you? And why have you deceived me?"

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לָבָ֔ן לֹא יֵעָשֶׂ֥ה כֵ֖ן בִּ/מְקוֹמֵ֑/נוּ לָ/תֵ֥ת הַ/צְּעִירָ֖ה לִ/פְנֵ֥י הַ/בְּכִירָֽה
verses.en: And Lavan said: "It is not done so in our place, to give the younger before the firstborn.

verses.he: מַלֵּ֖א שְׁבֻ֣עַ זֹ֑את וְ/נִתְּנָ֨ה לְ/ךָ֜ גַּם אֶת זֹ֗את בַּ/עֲבֹדָה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר תַּעֲבֹ֣ד עִמָּדִ֔/י ע֖וֹד שֶֽׁבַע שָׁנִ֥ים אֲחֵרֽוֹת
verses.en: Fulfill the week of this one, and we will give you this one also, for the service which you shall serve with me yet seven other years."

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֤עַשׂ יַעֲקֹב֙ כֵּ֔ן וַ/יְמַלֵּ֖א שְׁבֻ֣עַ זֹ֑את וַ/יִּתֶּן ל֛/וֹ אֶת רָחֵ֥ל בִּתּ֖/וֹ ל֥/וֹ לְ/אִשָּֽׁה
verses.en: And Yaakov did so, and fulfilled her week; and he gave him Rachel his daughter, to him as wife.

verses.he: וַ/יִּתֵּ֤ן לָבָן֙ לְ/רָחֵ֣ל בִּתּ֔/וֹ אֶת בִּלְהָ֖ה שִׁפְחָת֑/וֹ לָ֖/הּ לְ/שִׁפְחָֽה
verses.en: And Lavan gave to Rachel his daughter Bilhah his handmaid, to her as handmaid.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹא֙ גַּ֣ם אֶל רָחֵ֔ל וַ/יֶּאֱהַ֥ב גַּֽם אֶת רָחֵ֖ל מִ/לֵּאָ֑ה וַ/יַּעֲבֹ֣ד עִמּ֔/וֹ ע֖וֹד שֶֽׁבַע שָׁנִ֥ים אֲחֵרֽוֹת
verses.en: And he came in also unto Rachel, and he loved Rachel more than Leah; and he served with him yet seven other years.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֤רְא יְהוָה֙ כִּֽי שְׂנוּאָ֣ה לֵאָ֔ה וַ/יִּפְתַּ֖ח אֶת רַחְמָ֑/הּ וְ/רָחֵ֖ל עֲקָרָֽה
verses.en: And the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, and He opened her womb; but Rachel was barren.

verses.he: וַ/תַּ֤הַר לֵאָה֙ וַ/תֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֔ן וַ/תִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖/וֹ רְאוּבֵ֑ן כִּ֣י אָֽמְרָ֗ה כִּֽי רָאָ֤ה יְהוָה֙ בְּ/עָנְיִ֔/י כִּ֥י עַתָּ֖ה יֶאֱהָבַ֥/נִי אִישִֽׁ/י
verses.en: And Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuven — "See, a Son!" — for she said: "Because the LORD has seen my affliction; for now my husband will love me."

verses.he: וַ/תַּ֣הַר עוֹד֮ וַ/תֵּ֣לֶד בֵּן֒ וַ/תֹּ֗אמֶר כִּֽי שָׁמַ֤ע יְהוָה֙ כִּֽי שְׂנוּאָ֣ה אָנֹ֔כִי וַ/יִּתֶּן לִ֖/י גַּם אֶת זֶ֑ה וַ/תִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖/וֹ שִׁמְעֽוֹן
verses.en: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said: "Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, He has given me this one also." And she called his name Shimon — "Hearing."

verses.he: וַ/תַּ֣הַר עוֹד֮ וַ/תֵּ֣לֶד בֵּן֒ וַ/תֹּ֗אמֶר עַתָּ֤ה הַ/פַּ֨עַם֙ יִלָּוֶ֤ה אִישִׁ/י֙ אֵלַ֔/י כִּֽי יָלַ֥דְתִּי ל֖/וֹ שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה בָנִ֑ים עַל כֵּ֥ן קָרָֽא שְׁמ֖/וֹ לֵוִֽי
verses.en: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said: "Now this time my husband will be joined unto me, for I have borne him three sons." Therefore his name was called Levi — "Joining."

verses.he: וַ/תַּ֨הַר ע֜וֹד וַ/תֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֗ן וַ/תֹּ֨אמֶר֙ הַ/פַּ֨עַם֙ אוֹדֶ֣ה אֶת יְהוָ֔ה עַל כֵּ֛ן קָרְאָ֥ה שְׁמ֖/וֹ יְהוּדָ֑ה וַֽ/תַּעֲמֹ֖ד מִ/לֶּֽדֶת
